# ISSUES.md — No-go ledger for the Two-Boundary Retrocausal Unification

Living record of open problems, confirmed errors, conditional ceilings, and
contested points in `unification.tex`. (The former companions `Theorem.pdf` /
`Proof.pdf` are absorbed into the paper as of 2026-07-01 — see §F.) Curated
2026-06-26 from the 50-angle adversarial review, the round-2 implement/referee
pass, and the temporal-entanglement proof harness; updated 2026-07-01 from the
ten-angle literature audit and the theorem-repair/integration swarm.

**Status legend:** `OPEN` = unresolved · `FIX` = confirmed error, fix drafted ·
`COND` = claim true only under a stated condition · `WATCH` = contested /
referee-flagged, needs author judgment · `DONE` = resolved this session.

---

## A. Confirmed errors to fix (referee-vetted, drafts exist)

- **`DONE` Kisyński clock-uniqueness reduction is invalid.** (A29) The step
  $C=B+\id$ does **not** satisfy Kisyński's *homogeneous* operator-cosine
  equation, so the uniqueness argument in `thm:kisynski` did not go through as
  written. **Resolved 2026-07-01:** App. `app:kisynski` rewritten. Uniqueness
  now proved by symmetrizing the cocycle (swap $\delta_1\leftrightarrow\delta_2$
  + evenness ⇒ $\hat\Sigma_{\delta_1}B(\delta_2)=\hat\Sigma_{\delta_2}B(\delta_1)$,
  new `lem:wdw-swap`), which fixes the cos-*shape* fibrewise on the rational
  grid with *no* regularity input (stronger than the drafted Aczél re-route —
  the cosine kernel in (D) is known, so the cos/cosh trichotomy never arises);
  kernel block $\ker\hat H_c$ classified as $\delta^2 Q$ (parallelogram law)
  and killed by (E); fibre scale $g_\lambda=\alpha$ forced by (E) via direct
  integral + a.e.-subsequence extraction; rational→real by strong continuity.
  New explicit hypotheses: separability of $\mathcal H_{[E_\star]}$; (E) in
  second-order Peano form (E′); only $B(\delta)\in\{\hat H_c\}'$ needed from
  (B) (multiplicity handled — the $\{\hat H_c\}''$ reading is unnecessary).
  Also fixed: sign error in old `thm:kisynski` (cosine branch ↔ generator
  $-(\hat H_c/\hbar)^2\preceq 0$ in the $u''=Au$ convention); spurious
  "$(\alpha,\delta)\mapsto(-\alpha,-\delta)$" freedom removed ($\hat\Sigma$ is
  even in δ; α is unique when $\hat H_c\neq0$); ξ=0 boundedness non-sequitur in
  `prop:wdw-dalembert` re-routed through (E); unbounded-$\hat H_c$ extension
  (old residual item (i)) discharged (`rem:wdw-unbounded`). New verified refs:
  Fattorini, Indiana Univ. Math. J. 20 (1970) 411–425; Stetkær, *Functional
  Equations on Groups*, World Scientific 2013; Aczél 1966 pinpointed to §2.4.1.
- **`DONE` Trace-vs-KMS-state conflation in Hawking recovery.** (A32)
  **Resolved 2026-07-01:** Eq. `B5a-Bose-Einstein` now reads
  $\omega_{HH}(\hat N_\omega)$ (Hartle–Hawking KMS state, not the trace);
  `thm:B5a-Hawking` restated with "semifinite trace + KMS state"; equilibrium
  scope + surface-gravity circularity disclosed there and in the new
  `rem:step6-scope` (App. `app:tdse-derivation`). The Araki-perturbation first
  law `prop:step6-thermo` sharpens the benchmark: modified equilibrium at
  unrenormalised $T_H$, tilt $\Delta T/T_H=\mu E/2$, stability bound
  $\alpha<2\hbar^2/(\delta t^2E_\star)$ (non-tautological check).
- **`DONE` Modular-Hamiltonian band-limit non-sequitur.** (A35)
  **Resolved 2026-07-01** by `prop:E1-trace-finiteness`(iii) (App. B rewrite):
  the modular generator ($-\log\Delta_\omega$, state property) and the matter
  Hamiltonian (algebra generator) are now explicitly distinguished; the
  trace-finiteness supremum never saturates ($E_\star=\infty$ under the old
  `def:modular-cutoff`, which is deleted). See A5' below for the full repair.
- **`DONE` Numerical demo generator inconsistent.** (A33)
  **Resolved 2026-07-01:** `verify_U5_modifiedTDSE` rewritten — spurious
  $1/\delta^2$ removed, $H_{\rm mod}=H_c+\alpha\Sigma_\delta$ with
  $\alpha$ computed from the Born–Oppenheimer matching
  ($\mu=\alpha\delta t^2/\hbar^2=2c_0H_{\rm inf}/m_P^2$; KK $c_0=0.988$ /
  CK21 $c_0\approx1.5$); six checks incl. Born-invariance knitting with a
  mismatched-generator control (passes end-to-end, QuTiP 5.0.4).
  `schematic_U4` placeholder replaced by the matched $\mu$ with honest
  "illustrative / imported $c_0$" prints. Matching fixes ONLY the product
  $\mu$; $(\alpha,\delta t)$ split is convention — see the new `WATCH`
  μ-tension item in §B.
- **`DONE` Parity-odd signature: RETRACTED outright; replaced by the BAW
  laboratory falsifier.** (A34 + A48, resolved 2026-07-02 solver swarm.)
  The solver settled the physics question: $\Sigma_\delta$ **cannot source
  parity violation at all** — new `lem:sigma-parity` proves
  $[U_P,\hat H_c]=0$ via Tomita–Takesaki (parity-invariant seed ⇒ polar
  decomposition ⇒ $[U_P,\Delta_\omega]=0$), the correction is a real even
  function acting by functional calculus, helicity-blind on the tensor
  sector; every parity-odd correlator vanishes identically at all orders.
  §13.3 rebuilt: corrected parity-even `eq:pred-primordial-P` (with
  $(k_\star/k)^3$, $\log(-2k\eta)$, coefficient renamed $c_P$ — A48 done),
  amplitude-honesty remark (~10⁻⁹, unobservable), three-tier retraction
  remark `rem:parity-retraction` (kinematic: PO scalar bispectrum forbidden;
  dynamical: no PV vertex exists anywhere in the framework, and Appendix SM
  is deliberately parity-even; observational: BOSS anchor evaporated +
  DESI/kurto-spectra nulls, all now cited). New `sec:pred-baw`: the
  universal quadratic softening $\delta E=-\mu E^2/2$ promoted to designated
  laboratory falsifier — current bound $\mu\lesssim2.0\times10^{-24}$ eV⁻¹
  ($E_\star\gtrsim5.0\times10^{14}$ GeV, re-derived from Campbell et al.
  PRD 108, 102006 β₀<62.4, m_eff=8.5 mg), 4.4-decade window to $E_P$,
  universality/two-material discriminator free of the GUP soccer-ball
  ambiguity. Testability triage rewritten. *Ledger erratum fixed here:* the
  trispectrum no-go arXiv:2210.02907 is **Cabass–Jazayeri–Pajer–Stefanyszyn**
  (JHEP 02 (2023) 021), not Cabass–Ivanov–Philcox (= arXiv:2210.16320,
  PRD 107, 023523); both now cited correctly in the tex. Residual: the BAW
  β₀ fit is the hardening branch — a dedicated softening-sign re-fit would
  fix the O(1) factor; DESI/kurto-spectra refs are preprints (journal refs
  at proof stage).
- **`DONE` Appendix (QEI/E2) discharge rewritten: conditional, finite,
  scope-explicit.** (A49, resolved 2026-07-02 solver swarm; extends A26/A35
  family, both now moot.) App `app:E2-fewster-verch` fully rebuilt: the
  $Q^{1/2}$ ansatz and the infinite $\|\log\Delta\|$ summand are deleted with
  an explicit in-text retraction; `thm:E2-Lipschitz` now has the finite
  modulus `eq:E2-L` $=c[C_W+W_*C_{\mathcal D}+C_\Theta]$ sourced entirely
  from a strengthened (E2a) (`hyp:hadamard-uniform` upgraded to C²
  coincidence jets — the old C⁰ clause was silently insufficient for
  point-splitting); QEI input = Sanders' spacetime-smeared bound
  (`Sanders2024StressBounds`, CMP 405, 132 — key renamed, article number
  corrected from 131); modular input = Chialastri–Sanders energy-difference
  control (CMP 406, 285) via new `lem:E2-energy-lipschitz`; null components
  honestly scoped (Fewster–Roman free-field no-go; Fliss–Rolph QNEIs for
  interacting/2d; null leg stays entropic). `cor:E2-entropy-smallness` now
  yields the finite, strictly positive contraction `eq:E2-C-QFT` plus a NEW
  conditioned-source QEI floor `eq:E2-conditioned-floor` (feeds
  `rem:fs-achronal-anec`). (E2) status everywhere: "conditional theorem
  modulo (E2a)", quasi-free massive-scalar scope. New residuals recorded
  in-tex: (E2a) strengthened; (E2c) modular estimates beyond ultrastatic
  seeds; (E2b) Dirac/Maxwell; (E2d) interacting.

## A′′. 2026-07-02 (Opus) FIRST INDEPENDENT WAVE over the six-round repair layer
## (5 fresh maximum-effort agents, none authored the material they attacked).
## No FATAL to the conditional implication 𝗛 ⇒ unification. Defects CONVERGE
## on ONE deep issue (the A54 non-vacuity witness / CHT selection), plus one
## concrete proof bug, plus moderate null-shell geometry. Rounds 3–6 largely
## held. Convergent = three independent agents on the same core.

### >>> RESOLUTION (2026-07-02): A61–A71 ALL APPLIED. 212pp clean build, no
### undefined refs/citations. Three-agent design swarm (Opus) → sequential
### single-writer application (A63 → A61 → A62 → null-shell → axioms), recompile
### between regions. Key ledger self-correction below (A61). Details per item.
###
### A61 DONE — finite-dim non-vacuity decoupled from type-III. Added
###   prop:fd-nonvacuity (d=2, ρ=diag(¾,¼), σ_x PVM, f_±=0.118>0 by
###   mh-witness(iii) PROJECTIVE form — a THEOREM, no POVM converse). Witness
###   bullets in conj:master rewritten to point non-vacuity at the proposition,
###   not the infinite-dim POVM construction. rem:CHT-masa-open(i) downgraded.
###   **LEDGER CORRECTION**: the "ISR-genericity: POSITIVE obstruction" claim
###   in the A61 note BELOW was itself an overclaim. Research verdict: ISR
###   (Amrutam–Jiang etc.) is about Γ-invariant subalgebras of GROUP vN algebras
###   under discrete conjugation — does NOT transfer to a continuous ergodic
###   flow forbidding an invariant Cartan. [SUPERSEDED BY WAVE-2 BELOW: the
###   original claim here that "flow-invariant Cartans with Γ(α)=ℝ EXIST" cited
###   2605.02781 as Masuda–Tomatsu — that was a MISATTRIBUTION (real authors
###   Houdayer–Marrakchi) AND the paper is about OUTER flows, not Cartan-
###   preserving flows, so it does not support the claim. Corrected in wave-2:
###   the verdict is downgraded to "NOT SETTLED either way".
###   [FURTHER CORRECTED IN WAVE-3 (C5 below): the "leans toward obstructed"
###   framing was itself wrong. σ_t is INNER on 𝒜_cosm (Ad(λ_t), the crossed-
###   product centraliser Lemma), so the vise is the tractable CONJUGACY question
###   "does {λ_t} normalise a matter-crossing Cartan," not an exotic ergodic-flow
###   question; weak-mixing pertains to the type-III₁ boost on 𝒩, not the II₁
###   fixed-point structure. No obstruction known + Feldman–Moore normaliser
###   abundance ⟹ OPEN, NO KNOWN OBSTRUCTION, plausibly achievable. Lean flipped
###   from pessimistic to neutral/mild-optimistic.] So (Cartan ∧ flow-inv ∧
###   matter-supported) is genuinely OPEN. Non-vacuity of the PHENOMENON
###   (finite-dim) is independent of it.
### A62 DONE — new rem:CHT-aut-gauge: Aut(𝑅) ⊋ Out(𝑅) ⊋ finite QRF gauge;
###   forward QRF⟹conjugate proved, converse false; four "absorbed into Tier-(B)
###   gauge" sites restated to conjugacy-class uniqueness + open representative
###   selection. Wired into A61's rem:CHT-masa-open(i).
### A63 DONE — branch-I Petz element twisted to σ^ω_{−i/2}(Λ_k^{1/2}); (iv)
###   shield now holds because Σ_k Λ_k=id sits in the MIDDLE of the twisted
###   sandwich (not Σ_k L_k²=id). (i) proof, thm statement, finite-dim recovery,
###   and line-13025 interface all updated to L_k^*·L_k form; +AccardiCecchini1982.
### A64 DONE — headline is a CATEGORY-ERROR correction: the δ IS well-defined on
###   the Lipschitz record cone (Chruściel–Delay–Galloway–Howard: cut locus is
###   ℋ^{n-1}-null, codim ≥2). Shell supported on 𝒩_m^reg=𝒩_m∖𝒞_m; (vi-b)
###   restated as generator-affine transport up to the cut locus; caustic
###   amputated as new item (vi-b′).
### A65 DONE — new (vi-b″): non-crossing scope + Dray–'t Hooft–Redmount
###   composition rule (no new codim-2 matter in generic transverse crossing).
### A66 DONE — (E5) recast as the EXISTENCE condition for the C⁰/BV terminal-
###   limit metric; coincidence with g_K is the (x-c) matching, not automatic.
### A67 DONE — [C_AB]^TT=0 relabeled the physical no-radiation postulate (not
###   gauge), with the pure-trace+vector solvability caveat.
### A68 DONE — inline holographic/RT theorem-vs-generic-diamond-postulate stamp
###   in ax:E0-nl (ii)/(iii); "finite variations" phrasing; sign convention.
###   (Fallback citations used — LashkariVanRaamsdonk/FaulknerLi keys absent.)
### A69 DONE — new rem:speed-hierarchy: softened modes (v_g<c) vs exact-c shell
###   front; GW170817 preserved (photon+graviton same μ, cancels in difference).
### A70 DONE — delayed-choice scope remark in the (RP) block (spacelike deferred
###   choice is outside 𝒫_γ by construction; causal, not anticipating).
### A71 DONE — a: spurious E★⁻² removed (18c_L²·2^{−3n}, scaling unchanged);
###   b: Rindler edge-vs-interior parity split; c: (T5) eigenvalue-gap budget;
###   d: Unruh–Schützhold pointer in master-residual item (i). +5 null-shell
###   bibitems, +AmrutamJiang2022, +MasudaTomatsu2026 (all verified at source).

## WAVE 2 (2026-07-02) — 7-agent adversarial swarm on the A61–A71 repair layer.
## Verdict: master theorem 𝗛⇒(U1)–(U6) STRUCTURALLY INTACT (integration agent:
## every downgraded object is a hypothesis-in-𝗛 or already in the residual list;
## 0 smuggled hypotheses; prop:fd-nonvacuity exact; mh-witness(iii) genuine
## all-d projective theorem). All defects were LOCAL OVERCLAIMS OF STRENGTH in
## the new remarks — the fixes had removed the old overclaims but replaced them
## with new smaller affirmative overclaims. All 12 items below FIXED; 213pp
## clean build, no undefined refs/citations.
## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
## B1 `DONE` [SEVERE citation] MasudaTomatsu2026 bibitem: arXiv:2605.02781 is
##   real with exactly that title but authors are HOUDAYER–MARRAKCHI, not
##   Masuda–Tomatsu (confirmed by 2 independent agents at source). Worse, that
##   paper is about OUTER flows (Rohlin property), NOT Cartan-preserving flows —
##   so it never supported the "flow-invariant Cartan exists" claim it was cited
##   for. FIX: bibitem → HoudayerMarrakchi2026 (correct) + MasudaTomatsu2016
##   (the genuine Rohlin-flows Memoir, arXiv:1206.0955); rem:CHT-masa-open(i)
##   rewritten — "not positively obstructed" → "NOT SETTLED either way," the
##   affirmative flow-invariant-Cartan inference removed. [Lean further
##   corrected to neutral/mild-optimistic in wave-3 C5 — see below: σ_t inner.]
## B2 `DONE` [moderate] rem:CHT-aut-gauge overclaimed the FORWARD inclusion:
##   "QRF orientations act by flow-commuting automorphisms" is asserted, not
##   proved (prop:observer-invariance(B)/(D) give QRF-covariance, not flow-
##   commutativity). FIX: marked as EXPECTED/conjectural; only the converse's
##   falsity (⟹ "Aut(R) not gauge") is kept as unconditional.
## B3 `DONE` [minor–moderate] Same remark cited prop:horizon-alg-invariance for
##   "record LAW invariant under CFW class" — conflated the unconditional
##   MARGINAL isomorphism (part 1, generic, transfers to the II₁ factor) with
##   the (KE)-gated JOINT-with-K law (part 2, not established for a matter-
##   supported cosmological masa). FIX: split marginal vs joint; only the
##   marginal survives unconditionally; domain transfer flagged.
## B4 `DONE` [serious-latent] A67 TT=0: "mismatch DOES land in solvable subspace"
##   was folded into (vi-b) as if benign, but a generic radiative (TT) back-
##   reaction OBSTRUCTS EXISTENCE (not just uniqueness) of the null junction.
##   FIX: recast as a NAMED SOURCE-CLASS RESIDUAL scoped to 𝔓_UI (symmetric,
##   caustic-free → non-radiating by symmetry); outside 𝔓_UI the junction may
##   have no solution, disclaimed with (x-b), not asserted away.
## B5 `DONE` [moderate] A66 BV argument ran the junction map BACKWARDS: derived
##   metric-BV from finite shell-mass, but (vi-b) only bounds shell data ≤ C_J·
##   (metric jump) forward; the inverse (metric ≲ shell) fails (null junction
##   projects away TT, non-invertible). FIX: (E5) now posits the METRIC-INCREMENT
##   total variation directly; shell-mass finiteness stated as a necessary
##   companion, not a sufficient substitute.
## B6 `DONE` [moderate] A65 "no new codim-2 matter in generic transverse
##   crossing" was flat-asserted; caustic-crossing (non-transverse, DTR
##   hypothesis fails) fell through every net. FIX: made it a named assumption
##   parallel to (vi-b′); crossing-at-a-cut-locus explicitly disclaimed
##   (void in 𝔓_UI).
## B7 `DONE` [moderate] A64 dressed the "no codim-2 strut" assumption as a
##   codimension CONSEQUENCE ("its codimension exceeds that of a thin shell") and
##   called the integrable-transverse-curvature condition "equivalently" the
##   no-strut condition. FIX: codim-2 struts ARE exactly codim-2 (excluded by
##   assumption, not automatically); "equivalently" → "sufficient condition,"
##   noting generic focusing drives transverse curvature → −∞.
## B8 `DONE` [minor] Stale prose: "(vi) Two ingredients" (now four items);
##   "Combined, (vi-a) and (vi-b)" summary. FIX: → four ingredients / "(vi-a)–
##   (vi-b″)", L∞(𝒩_m^reg), scoped to certified class.

##   set by Ĥ_m spectra + non-resonance. FIX: re-sourced to the constraint

## B10 `DONE` [moderate] A63 5a (my own wave-1 fix): eq:E4-vector-rep claimed
##   ξ_k = p(k)^{−1/2}L_kΩ as the natural-cone rep, but the twist pushes L_kΩ OFF

##   note L_kΩ induces the same STATE but isn't the cone vector (= only up to

## B11 `DONE` [low] A63 5b (my own wave-1 fix): the KMS rewrite in the (iv) proof

## WAVE 3 (2026-07-02) — 6-agent adversarial swarm on the B-series repair layer,
## the master-theorem SPINE, the deepest open item (flow-vise), citations, and

## C1 `DONE` [HIGH, consistency] Abstract line 205 said two-level toy gives
##   C≥1/8 nats — stale PRE-Pinsker-correction value; body computes 1/32 (×3).
##   FIX: abstract → C≥1/32.

##   "(E0)+(E1)–(E4)" but E4 is closed unconditionally and E1→H0. FIX: residual is
##   (E0)+(E2a)+(E3a); E1 retired to H0, E4 closed, E2/E3 reduced to E2a/E3a.
## C3 `DONE` [MED, master-thm logic] (U6) in thm:master-unification read as if ONE
##   de Sitter witness gave BOTH scope-non-emptiness AND phenomenon f_k>0 —
##   contradicting my own wave-2 witness-block edit (8185–8201) that severs them.

##   proof + rem cross-updated.
## C4 `DONE` [MED, master-thm logic] "𝗛⇒(U1)–(U6) unconditional" obscured that
##   BUNDLE SATISFIABILITY at cosmological scale is itself open (kill-test outcome
##   (i) ⟹ ax:complete (in H1) unsatisfiable there ⟹ H vacuous THERE). FIX: added
##   honesty clause to rem:conditional-unification — unconditional AS AN
##   IMPLICATION; lab-scale satisfiable unconditionally (prop:fd-nonvacuity);

## C5 `DONE` [MED, flow-vise — the big one] The vise was mischaracterized as an
##   exogenous weakly-mixing ergodic flow. σ_t is INNER on 𝒜_cosm (Ad(λ_t),
##   crossed-product centraliser Lemma), so clause (B) = "the ONE unitary group
##   {λ_t} normalises a Cartan 𝓜, acting non-trivially" — a tractable CONJUGACY
##   question, NOT the exotic ergodic-flow question. Weak-mixing / full-Connes-
##   spectrum pertains to the type-III₁ boost on 𝒩, NOT the II₁ fixed-point
##   structure (whose relative commutant is large). No obstruction known;
##   Feldman–Moore normaliser generates 𝑅 ⟹ flow-inv+matter-supported Cartans
##   exist in abundance FOR SUITABLE flows; open gap is only whether the GIVEN
##   {λ_t} is one. FIX: rewrote rem:CHT-masa-open with inner-flow reframing +
##   conjugacy question + Feldman–Moore (+bibitem FeldmanMoore1977, verified);
##   status → "OPEN, NO KNOWN OBSTRUCTION, plausibly achievable." Lean FLIPPED
##   from pessimistic to mild-optimistic. Blast radius (if ever obstructed):
##   quarantined to cosmological II₁ arm; prop:fd-nonvacuity, master thm, falsifier
##   all survive — confirmed correct by the vise agent.
## C6 `DONE` [MED] (H0)/(H3)/(H4) not co-scoped: (H0) collapses type-III to
##   finite-dim, but (H3)/(H4) are predicates ABOUT type-III. FIX: added
##   co-scoping clause to (H0) — matter sector compressed; clock/gravity/horizon
##   retain type-III/crossed-product; (H3)/(H4) asserted on the uncompressed
##   algebra, finite-dim reductions on Ran(P≤E★), related by Π not identified.
## C7 `DONE` [MED, completing wave-2 B2/B3] Upstream "Why Cartan" prose (17420–24)
##   still stated categorically "The forward inclusion HOLDS — QRF orientations
##   act by flow-commuting automorphisms," contradicting the hedged rem:CHT-aut-
##   gauge. FIX: softened to "expected (not proved)"; marginal-law invariance
##   correctly attributed to prop:horizon-alg-invariance(1).
## C8 `DONE` [MED, completing wave-2 B4] The no-radiation postulate's "non-
##   radiating by symmetry" was scoped to 𝔓_UI, but 𝔓_UI is STATISTICAL (QND/
##   likelihood-gap) with parallel-caustic-free geometry — NOT spherical symmetry,
##   does NOT kill the quadrupole. FIX: recast as a genuine QUADRUPOLE-FREE source-
##   class residual explicitly NOT implied by 𝔓_UI (generic 𝔓_UI records radiate ⟹
##   junction obstructed); certified sub-class is strictly narrower (Birkhoff).
## C9 `DONE` [MED, completing wave-2 B5] Prose renamed (E5)'s load-bearing quantity
##   to metric-increment TV while the formal (E5)/(x-b) statement stayed shell-mass.
##   FIX (corrected further than the agent asked): on the certified no-TT class the
##   shell data IS the full [C_AB] jump, so shell-mass = metric-derivative-jump TV
##   = the C⁰/BV condition (junction map bi-Lipschitz, non-invertible direction =
##   the TT part that vanishes there). Formal statement + prose aligned to this
##   equivalence — no bogus inverse bound needed.
## C10 `DONE` [LOW, physical candor] Falsifier framing foregrounded "two-channel
##   near-term testability"; matched-branch magnitude is 4 decades below reach.
##   FIX: foregrounded SIGN+LINEARITY+UNIVERSALITY (form) as the live content, not
##   magnitude; noted BAW channel bites only modulo the no-cancellation assumption.
## C11 `DONE` [B10/B11 re-verified] My wave-2 E4-twist fixes (eq:E4-vector-rep,

## GR-DERIVATION (upstream, 10-agent campaign) `CLOSED-NOGO w/ upgrade path`:
##   the Einstein EQUATION cannot be derived non-circularly. HKT-UNIQUENESS AND
##   ANOMALY-IMMUNITY ARE ONE COIN (2026-07-02 route-4 verdict): the 2511.07753
##   anomaly says the semiclassical hypersurface-deformation algebra CANNOT close;
##   HKT uniqueness is a theorem ABOUT that algebra's closure. The framework is
##   IMMUNE to the anomaly precisely BECAUSE it forgoes the deformation algebra
##   (finite-dim H0 + abelian Page-Wootters constraint {C,C}=0 + per-branch definite
##   geometry, on-shell field eq not off-shell constraint algebra) — and that SAME
##   choice makes HKT uniqueness UNAVAILABLE. Mutually exclusive by construction: no
##   config enjoys closure-forces-Einstein AND anomaly-immunity together; the paper
##   (correctly) claims NEITHER as its Einstein derivation. HKT meets ZERO of its 4
##   hypotheses here ⟹ NOT a viable demotion route. (HKT Annals Phys 96 (1976) 88;
##   Husain-Javed 2511.07753 anomaly is 1st-order-in-⟨T⟩ via state-on-metric dep, NOT
##   variance/coherence — so branch-decoherence doesn't "suppress" it, structure does.
##   Halliwell per-branch-Einstein: attribute to broader WKB program PRD36 3626 (1987)
##   + 0909.2566, not the 1989 decoherence paper alone.)
##   Prior notes retained: modular/ANEC area↔boost identity is the
##   RAYCHAUDHURI-INTEGRATED FORM of the null-null (vv) Einstein constraint GIVEN
##   C_l=0 as input (Faulkner-Speranza 2405.00847 fn3 verbatim: C_l "a component of
##   the Einstein equation" is IMPOSED, not derived) — it CONSUMES that one component,
##   does NOT produce the full tensor eq [corrected 2026-07-02: prior "IS the Einstein
##   constraint" shorthand OVERSTATED it]; entanglement-equilibrium is
##   MVEH⟺Einstein biconditional; causal-consistency necessary-not-sufficient;
##   two-boundary content can't source gravity; ER=EPR forbidden by design). BEST
##   available: linear (thm:E0-lin, have) + n=2 (Faulkner2017+Lashkari-VR, bankable)
##   as THEOREMS; swap E0's Jacobson-Clausius postulate for the QFC-saturation /
##   rel-entropy-monotonicity primitive (already load-bearing in ANEC+thermal time

## MATTER-SPECTRUM DERIVATION (2026-07-02, 5-agent survey) `CLOSED-NOGO`:
##   "Strength of gravity" = the dimensionless α_G = the LIGHTNESS OF MATTER = the
##   FLAVOR PUZZLE. Can the matter spectrum be derived? NO — not by anyone, and the
##   framework PROVABLY can't (which is correct, by design). Verified at primary
##   source across 5 routes:
##   • Koide (m_e+m_μ+m_τ)/(√..)²=2/3 to 1e-5 (tau-limited): REAL coincidence but a
##     3→2 RELATION not a derivation; fails for quarks (Q_up≈0.89); orthogonal to
##     the framework (the Weinberg-as-thermal-time analogy does NOT transfer — Koide
##     has no thermodynamic/horizon content).
##   • Froggatt-Nielsen: relocates hierarchy to U(1) charges + ε; O(1)s remain free.
##   • Modular flavor (Feruglio): CHAMPION reduction — 2 real (Re τ, Im τ) → 8 ν
##     observables — but τ is FIT, and it assumes group+weights+reps+Kähler+SUSY.
##     FN = large-Im-τ cusp limit of modular flavor (ε=e^{2πiτ}); two faces of one
##     relocation. τ-prediction needs moduli stabilization = trades fit-τ for fit-flux.
##   • GUTs: minimal SO(10) fits 18 observables with 29 params (MORE params than
##     data, Babu-Bajc-Saad 1612.04329); b-τ unification works (1 relation);
##     Georgi-Jarlskog Clebsch is SELECTED to fit. RELATE, not derive.
##   • Asymptotic safety: Shaposhnikov-Wetterich pre-LHC Higgs=126 GeV is a real but
##     SOFT prediction (sign choice + no-new-physics + top-mass-sensitive → 132 GeV
##     w/ updated m_t); Eichhorn-Held top=171 GeV truncation-fragile. A FEW contested
##     predictions, not the spectrum. AND foreclosed anyway: H0 is an explicit
##     "falsifiable bet AGAINST the Reuter fixed point" (lines 7206-7230), and AS
##     needs the trans-Planckian regime H0 excises. (GUT relations, sub-Planckian,
##     remain technically available — but that imports a GUT, not derives natively.)
##   THE MODULAR PUN — CLOSED: modular-flavor SL(2,ℤ) (fractional-linear on a
##     compactification modulus τ, holomorphic modular FORMS) ≠ Tomita-Takesaki
##     Δ_ω^{it} (modular AUTOMORPHISM group, flow=ℝ/KMS, positive self-adjoint
##     operator). Real bridge only in the CFT-torus corner (torus τ = inverse-temp +
##     angular potential, Cardy modular invariance IS KMS/Tomita) — but that does NOT
##     reach flavor. Framework's OWN rem:spectral-triple-blindness (Connes uniqueness:
##     generation-sector A_F enters as INNER autos ⟹ invisible to thermal-time flow)
##     forecloses it from the framework side. Do NOT claim modular flow explains
##     flavor — category error.
##   HONEST TERMINUS (already in the paper, licensed by rem:spectral-triple-blindness
##     + App SM "accommodation"): the framework takes matter as INPUT by design, a
##     PROVED domain boundary (not a gap), status identical to the SM's ~19-26
##     measured flavor params — exactly as GR takes T_μν. This CLOSES the entire
##     "derive the strength of gravity" thread. Nothing to fix; optionally state the
##     one-paragraph terminus (skeptic drafted it) a bit more prominently.

### THE CONVERGENT CORE — witness non-vacuity / Cartan-flow (R1). SEVERE.
- **A61 `DONE` — the A54 non-vacuity witness is not established** (two-branch
  referee + saboteur atk6 + interlocks with A63). Three stacked links, all
  unproven: (i) `thm:mh-witness`(iii) is strictly FINITE-DIM and
  PROJECTIVE-effect; its clean f_k>0 ⟺ [ρ,Λ]≠0 FAILS for POVM effects (paper's
  own d=2 counterexample). The witness's terminal effects are spectral windows
  of Πφ̂(f)Π compressed again onto 𝓗_[E*] — generically a POVM, not a
  projector — so [ρ_i,Λ_k]≠0 does NOT license f_k>0. The "non-vacuous in
  phenomenon" payload is UNSUPPORTED. (ii) Πφ̂(f)Π (unbounded field compressed
  by a non-commuting projection) is only symmetric, not shown self-adjoint /
  affiliated to the II₁ corner — spectral windows not guaranteed to exist.
  (iii) THE VISE (converged, saboteur atk6): CHT clause (B) needs the terminal
  masa flow-invariant; the witness needs effects NOT flow-commuting (else
  f_k≡0). Cartan∧matter-nontrivial ✓; Cartan∧flow-invariant ✗ (ISR-genericity:
  ergodic flows generically forbid invariant Cartan masas — a POSITIVE
  obstruction, not neutral-open); flow-invariant∧matter = the energy masa,
  matter-TRIVIAL. CFW gives Cartan up-to-Aut but the matter-injecting
  conjugating automorphism is generically NOT flow-commuting ⟹ breaks (B).
  Round 1 relocated this into the "open kill-test" rather than resolving it.
  HONEST FIX (not a quick one): downgrade "non-vacuous in phenomenon" to
  conjectural; upgrade rem:CHT-masa-open(i) / rem:CHT-kill-test from "open" to
  "obstructed-pending-resolution (ISR-genericity)"; state that joint
  satisfiability of (Cartan ∧ flow-(B) ∧ matter-nontrivial) is the sharpest
  structural risk and Ax.complete's cosmological realizability rides on it.
- **A62 `DONE` — Aut(𝑅) "gauge" overclaim** (two-branch referee, major): CFW
  residual freedom is Aut(hyperfinite II₁) — enormous, contains Out(𝑅) — NOT
  the finite-parameter QRF frame gauge of prop:observer-invariance(B). The
  "uniqueness up to gauge" is really "up to Aut(𝑅)", which is not gauge. Prove
  only QRF ⟹ conjugate; the converse (conjugate ⟹ QRF-related) is false.
  Restate the CFW-absorption claim honestly.

### THE CONCRETE PROOF BUG — fixable, physics survives.
- **A63 `DONE` — branch-I prior-average shield uses the wrong Petz element**
  (saboteur atk3, numerically confirmed; the single most dangerous attack).
  `thm:E4-dual-weight`(iv) claims Σ_k p(k)ω_k = ω "by Σ_k L_k²=id" with the
  UNTWISTED L_k=Λ_k^{1/2} — but that gives density Λ_k^{1/2}ρΛ_k^{1/2}, whose
  prior average is Φ*(ρ) ≠ ρ (counterexample ρ=diag(¾,¼), x-basis effects →
  ½·id, full Bloch collapse). Inconsistent with clause (iii) of the SAME
  theorem, which uses the modular twist. FIX (verified): redefine the branch-I
  Petz element in the App E4 Setup (~15781) and the (iv) proof (~15992) as the
  modular-twisted L_k = σ^{ω̂}_{−i/2}(Λ_k^{1/2}) = ρ^{1/2}Λ_k^{1/2}ρ^{−1/2}
  (exactly branch-II's element, ~4505); then density = ρ^{1/2}Λ_kρ^{1/2}/p and
  Σ_k p(k)ω_k = ρ EXACTLY. This is the load-bearing no-signalling identity for
  the branch that carries all matter content — must land before the branch-I
  discrimination defense is airtight.

### MODERATE — null-shell geometry (R2), all funneled into (vi-b).
- **A64 `DONE` — caustic ill-definedness** (null-shell referee, strongest there):
  past the cut locus ∂J⁺(γ(s_m)) is not a smooth embedded null hypersurface —
  it self-intersects, generators leave it — so the surface delta δ_{𝒩_m} in
  eq:fs-einstein-distrib and the shell stress are UNDEFINED there. (vi-b)
  converts a geometric ill-definedness of the field equation into an analytic
  existence assumption — a category error. Needs an amputation-at-cut-locus
  prescription (achronal boundary up to the cut locus + separate caustic-set
  treatment), not just "(vi-b) is a hypothesis."
- **A65 `DONE` — crossing shells unaddressed** (null-shell referee): successive
  cones intersect; shell collisions generically produce extra content
  (Dray–'t Hooft); the global-weak-solution claim is unverified at crossings
  and the case is never mentioned.
- **A66 `DONE` — terminal accumulation** (null-shell referee + saboteur atk5):
  BLOCKED by (E5)/(x-b) summability + (x-c) matching (saboteur confirms the
  τ_f-limit metric exists in the C⁰/BV class under the uniformly-informative
  class) — but (E5) is load-bearing for the EXISTENCE of the terminal-limit
  metric, not merely ball-containment; the paper frames (E5) as containment
  only. State (E5) as the existence condition; the outside-𝔓_UI scope wall is
  honestly declared.
- **A67 `DONE` — TT-selection mislabeled as gauge** (null-shell referee): the
  zero-impulsive-wave [C_AB]^TT=0 choice is sold as a gauge choice but
  functions as a PHYSICAL hypothesis (measurement records emit no gravitational
  radiation); (vi-b) silently assumes the constraint mismatch lands in the
  TT=0 subspace. Relabel as a physical no-radiation hypothesis; note the
  solvability obstruction.

### CLARITY / MINOR (add remarks; mostly one-liners).

- **A70 `DONE` — delayed-choice-eraser scope remark** (saboteur atk2, BLOCKED

- **A71 `DONE` — minor**: qubit-scaling formula spurious E★⁻² (cosmetic, 2^{−3n}
  unaffected); "every point-centred parity" over-quantifier in the Rindler
  sentence; "area = EE to all orders" phrasing → "finite variations of";

### VERIFIED / POSITIVES (survived the independent wave).
- pos:geom-nosig(iv) VERIFIED; ALL signalling attacks BLOCKED — the
  covariantized architecture is "as causal as electromagnetism" (timing

- Fresh unlisted-hypothesis hunt: NEGATIVE — no new master-chain-consuming

- E4 algebraic scaffolding for BOUNDED strictly-positive effects: sound

  CFW hyperfiniteness/conjugacy-type chain correct (automorphism, not inner).

Original findings of the wave (kept for the record; all now resolved as
summarized above):

- **`FIX` A53 — Record-adapted sourcing layer needs major revision** (saboteur
  + record-referee, converged). (i) CAUSALITY: record shells on full Cauchy
  slices ⟹ the metric at spacelike separation from a record event is
  record-dependent at O(κ) — protocol choice/timing modulates the DISTRIBUTION
  (not mean) of distant metric observables ⟹ one-shot signalling. The
  Barrabès–Israel NULL-SHELL covariantization (condition g(x) only on records
  in J⁻(x)) is LOAD-BEARING, not a refinement; drags with it multi-worldline
  record structure (two-observer metric disagreement is otherwise derivable
  from the paper's own props) and a record-permanence principle (quantum-eraser
  attack: monotone filtration vs reversible records ⟹ metric which-path
  memory ⟹ zero Wigner-revival prediction at any coupling — needs
  terminally-readable-records admissibility clause) and a gravitational
  pointer-basis principle (Wigner-friend-with-gravimeter fork: gravitate-and-
  be-irreversible vs reversible-and-not-gravitate, decided by 𝒫_γ). (ii)
  WELL-DEFINEDNESS: ρ₂(τ|k) needs Λ_k transported through the FUTURE metric —
  not F̄_τ-measurable without an unstated continuation convention on g_r
  beyond the last record; π_τ defined only for rational τ; trivial-protocol
  clause assumes p₀ where (x-c)'s p is operative. (iii) SHELLS: constraint
  mismatch (4 fns) underdetermines [K_ab] (6 fns) — "the unique Israel
  solution" needs an unstated selection rule; eq:fs-einstein-shell uses the
  TIMELIKE Lanczos sign for SPACELIKE slices (ε=n·n missing); Israel
  compatibility relations (D_aS^a_b = −[T^n_b] etc.) unverified for arbitrary
  posterior jumps; shelled g_ω never checked against the achronal-ANEC
  admissibility layer; shells accumulate at τ_f where Israel is a finite-shell
  formalism. (iv) (x-a): the record tree has NO seed gap (inf P(r) = 0 with
  depth) while the paper's own Lipschitz constants scale as (sector prob)⁻¹ —
  in tension with its own scaling analysis; (x-b): ℓ²⇏ℓ¹, and continuous
  monitoring gives a.s. infinite total variation — the L² identity is
  compatible with a.s. failure; the "O(κ²) protocol-independence bound in
  hypothesis (x)" cited by rem:geom-nosig-status DOES NOT EXIST in (x). (v)
  (x-d) POVM-stability hypothesis needed (CHT selection on which geometry? —
  the paper's own δΛ variance makes 𝒜_cosm sector-relative); terminal-shell
  information budget remark needed (when Ax complete fails the shell broadcasts
  H(K|F̄) bits — GSL bookkeeping absent). (vi) Minor: worked example's forward
  state subnormalized between epochs; PG "exactly the behavior reported" and
  "classical mixture" overclaims; shells are C⁰-not-C¹ ("invisible to open-set
  observation" false as stated). Repair drafts exist in the saboteur/referee
  reports (record-permanence hypothesis, (x-d), info-budget remark, relational-
  status remark, null-shell program).
- **`FIX` A54 — Centralizer relocation: scope disjoint from content** (algebra
  referee; exact computation). On the centralizer-rich scope ω_k↾𝒩 = ω for
  EVERY branch (type-I model + general weight-KMS identity): conditioning =
  classical Bayesian updating of clock energy; records carry zero matter
  information; in the dS witness every operator is a function of the boost ⟹
  [ρ,Λ_k] ≡ 0 ⟹ MH witness f_k ≡ 0 — the two-boundary link is operationally
  invisible exactly on the conjecture's scope. Repairs: matter datum ≠ seed
  inside conj:tomita, or honest merger with the non-(CP) Araki branch (E4,
  which needs no centralizer — note the E4/hyp:centralizer double-counting:
  the paper both assumes and bypasses (CP)), or trace-conditioning on ΠÑΠ
  (severs link to ω). Also: dual-weight bookkeeping ill-posed (canonical CE
  E_𝒩: Ñ→𝒩 does not exist — Haagerup operator-valued weight has T(1)=∞; ω̂
  does not restrict to ω; branch weights on unprojected Ñ non-normalizable:
  Σp(k) = ω̂(1) = ∞); "type II∞ subfactor" classifications at ~4619/4665/4808
  contradict the Lemma (centralizer contains {λ_s}″ ⊆ its center — NEVER a
  factor); sweep-miss at ~4773 ("extremal ⟹ commutative" — false, refuted by
  the paper's own Haagerup cite); Lemma proof's spectral-subspace spanning
  step needs Landstad duality (statement TRUE, proof fixable); −logΔ_ω̂ ∉ Ñ

- **`FIX` A55 — E2 rewrite quantitative defects** (E2 referee). Pinsker

- **`FIX`/`WATCH` A57 — Photon dispersion vs exact-LI claim: the
  Planck-natural branch is DEAD** (3 agents converged: CPT/QFT, precision-lab,
  gravity). If free photons inherit the universal softening (the paper's own
  "every mode, every material" argument provides no exemption), v_g = c(1−μE)
  = linear LIV with E_QG,1 = 1/μ. GRB 090510 (E_QG,1 > 7.6 E_P) and LHAASO
  GRB 221009A (> 10 E_P — CITED BY THE PAPER at App lorentz-bounds) then give
  μ < 8×10⁻³⁰ eV⁻¹: (a) μ_PN = 1/E_P EXCLUDED ×7.6–10; (b) the advertised
  4.4-decade BAW window collapses by 5.4 decades — photon timing, not phonon
  cavities, is the binding constraint; (c) prop:lorentz-bounds "ξ=η=0 at all
  orders" and prop:tdse-effective cannot both be unrestricted (the LI proof
  covers only the thermal-time sector). A50 is hereby RESOLVED EMPIRICALLY in
  favor of the BO-matched branch (μ ~ 10⁻³³ eV⁻¹, survives by ~3–4 decades).
  Required: either scope Σ_δ to matter/bound sectors with a stated reason
  photons differ from BAW phonons, or carve out prop:lorentz-bounds and adopt
  GRB time-of-flight as the leading falsifier; rewrite sec:pred-baw reach/
  triage accordingly. RELATED (gravity agent): MICROSCOPE η < 4×10⁻¹⁵ kills
  the rest-mass-inclusive reading of Ĥ_s outright (whole-body: excluded ≥16
  orders for every candidate μ; per-atom: would force μ < 5.8×10⁻²⁶, 35×
  tighter than BAW) — the excitation reading is FORCED, not conventional; add
  the remark (free consistency win). RELATED (precision-lab): the universality
  claim needs a validity ceiling — under the (α,δt)=(E_P,t_P) split the exact
  cosine correction saturates at O(1) for macroscopic coherent modes (Earth

- **`WATCH` A56 — conj:CHT-POVM graded IMPLAUSIBLE as stated; Cartan/CFW
  repair route identified** (time-sector + masa research). Clauses (B),(C)
  are VACUOUS in the II₁ idealisation (trace centralizer = whole algebra ⟹
  only conditional expectation is identity; (C) trivial); "uniqueness up to
  record-process equivalence" is trivially true (all diffuse masas ≅

  Pukánszky invariant; uncountable non-conjugate singular families; conjugacy

  FORBIDS invariant masas. REPAIR: 𝒜_cosm is hyperfinite (injective) ⟹
  Connes–Feldman–Weiss gives Cartan-masa uniqueness up to conjugacy — restate
  clause (A) as CARTAN masa, absorb Aut freedom into the Tier-(B) QRF gauge,
  motivate Cartan-ness as record objectivity/redundancy (normalizer-rich),
  restate (B) against the horizon flow not the trace. KILL TEST (decidable):

  must be done together.

- **`FIX` A58 — ax:E0-nl formulation defect** (gravity-sector assessor): the

## B. Conditional / scoped claims (honest ceilings)

- **`DONE` Planck cutoff (E1): no Planck threshold actually emerges.** (A5)
  **Resolved 2026-07-01 — confirmed, and worse than logged, then repaired.**
  The audit found (i) the old `thm:E1-modular-cutoff` was *false under its own
  definition* ($\int_0^E e^{-2\pi E'/\kappa}dE'<\infty$ for every $E$, so
  $E_\star=\infty$; the Planck scale entered only via a by-hand shift
  $\hat H_R\to K+E_P\id$); (ii) it rested on **phantom citations** — "CLPW
  Thm. 3.1" and "DEHK Prop. 4.2" do not exist, and the "Faulkner–Speranza
  double-scaling trace representation" appears nowhere in arXiv:2405.00847;
  (iii) the II$_1$/II$_\infty$ inconsistency is a direct contradiction with
  the cited sources (positive clock / compact diamond ⟹ II$_1$ per
  CLPW/DEHK §2.1/JSS; "II$_\infty$ with finite trace" is an oxymoron).
  **Repair applied:** App. B rewritten — `def:modular-cutoff` and
  `thm:E1-modular-cutoff` deleted; new `prop:E1-trace-finiteness` states what
  IS provable (II$_1$: finite trace + max-entropy tracial state; II$_\infty$:
  τ-finite half-line bands, τ(1)=∞; no threshold in either case); (E1)
  retired, `pos:planck-cutoff` restated as explicit Wilsonian postulate H0
  (`rem:E1-status`). Type classification corrected paper-wide (App. F ⟹ II$_1$
  with II$_\infty$ variant for unbounded-charge sectors; App. I finite-Λ
  members II$_1$; App. O type-agnostic with II$_1$ strengthening
  `rem:RAQ-II1` — normalisability now automatic; §13.1 dynamical-horizon
  II$_\infty$ **legitimate** per Chen–Penington arXiv:2406.02116, with the new
  two-sector reconciliation `rem:page-type-reconciliation`: II$_1$ lab corner
  of II$_\infty$ horizon algebra). Uniqueness outline rewritten to match
  (II$_1$ strengthens the theorem: trace normalisation kills the scale
  freedom). H0 remains `OPEN` as a postulate (see §C).
- **`DONE` "Covariant conservation = Tomita KMS" — split applied.** (A6,
  resolved 2026-07-02 solver swarm.) Worse than logged: the old proof's KMS
  step used $B=\id$, a tautology deriving nothing. Now split:
  `prop:conservation-as-KMS` = KMS stationarity (invariance, generator
  identity with corrected sign per `eq:thermal-time` convention, geometric
  Lie-derivative form, explicit scope disclaimer), and new
  `lem:semiclassical-conservation` = $\nabla^\mu\langle\hat T_{\mu\nu}
  \rangle_\omega=0$ for EVERY Hadamard state by Wald/Moretti renormalisation
  (Moretti CMP 232 Thm 2.1(a); operator-level D=4 Thm 3.2(c) = free-field
  form of `eq:consv-axiom`; Wald PRD 17, 1477 counterterm; trace-anomaly-
  only caveat; interacting via Hollands–Wald RMP 17, 227; Dirac via
  Dappiaggi–Hack–Pinamonti RMP 21, 1241) — independent of KMS. Five
  downstream sites rewired, including the honest weakening of the
  H-shrink bullet (`eq:consv-axiom` on the dynamical background remains a
  hypothesis). All pinpoints verified at source; BR II deliberately cited
  at chapter level.
- **`COND` (E2) Lipschitz step has a dimensional gap.** (A26) A worldline scalar
  $Q$ is not directly the lower bound for a general smeared tensor component
  $T_{\mu\nu}[f]$; the $Q^{1/2}$ step is asserted, not derived.
- **`COND` `thm:E0-lin` is AdS-only.** (A43) The linearised-Einstein-from-
  entanglement leg holds in asymptotically-AdS; bridge to the beyond-AdS
  holographic-entropy program or scope the claim explicitly.
- **`COND` Acceleration-horizon / DSW prediction is unobservably small.** (A44)
  $\Gamma_{\rm DSW}(\kappa)$ for terrestrial BMV is a matter of principle, not a
  near-term distinctive prediction; downgrade accordingly.
- **`WATCH` μ-scaling tension: cutoff convention vs BO matching.** (A50, new
  2026-07-01; recorded in-tex as `rem:mu-tension`.) `rem:cutoff-TDSE`'s
  normalization convention ($\alpha\sim E_\star$, $\delta t\sim\hbar/E_\star$
  ⟹ $\mu\sim1/E_\star$) and the Born–Oppenheimer matching
  ($\mu=2c_0H_{\rm inf}/m_P^2\approx10^{-5}/m_P$) differ by a factor
  $\sim H_{\rm inf}/m_P$; both cannot hold. Author decision: which is the
  physical normalization? Empirical status: the phonon-cavity
  (BAW/GUP) bound gives $\mu\lesssim2\times10^{-24}\,$eV⁻¹
  ($E_\star=1/\mu\gtrsim5\times10^{14}$ GeV, Campbell et al. PRD 108, 102006);
  both candidate values sit far below it. Related opportunity: the
  amplitude–frequency "softening" channel ($\delta E_n=-\mu E_n^2/2$,
  universal across modes/materials, free of the GUP soccer-ball ambiguity) is
  the framework's best falsifier — a drafted "falsifiable consequences"
  section exists from the 2026-07-01 swarm and could be added to the paper.
- **`DONE` CLPW centralizer misattribution — Route-A relocation applied.**
  (A52, resolved 2026-07-02 solver swarm.) The records $\{E_k\}$ are
  relocated to the dressed crossed-product centralizer
  (`eq:tomita-Ek-physical`: $E_k\in\widetilde{\mathcal N}^{\sigma^{\hat\omega}}$),
  with a new theorem-grade Lemma
  ($\widetilde{\mathcal N}^{\sigma^{\hat\omega}}
  =\mathcal N^{\sigma^\omega}\rtimes\RR \supseteq L^\infty(\RR)$, proof via
  dual-action spectral subspaces — EQUALITY, strengthened during
  application) making the scope unconditionally non-vacuous for every
  faithful normal seed. Standing hypothesis renamed **centralizer-rich
  scope** (the old "non-extremal ⟺ nontrivial centralizer" equivalence was
  itself wrong: extremality ⟺ factor state; Haagerup's state is extremal
  WITH II₁ centralizer; the KMS simplex is governed by the CENTER of the
  centralizer, BR II §5.3). Regime split now honest: (a) centralizer-rich
  abstract seeds (Haagerup Acta Math. 158 Thm 3.1; Connes 1973/1974 —
  undressed records possible); (b) geometric KMS seeds (Borchers–Buchholz:
  weakly mixing ⇒ trivial 𝒩-centralizer — dressed records only, which is
  where App J always lived). Corrective remark `rem:tomita-Ek-relocation`
  records the retraction. Bonus fixes: latent instance of the same error in
  `prop:clock-automatic`(ii) corrected; two stale sites beyond the planned
  sweep caught (rem:thermal-time-scope, App E4 (CP)-specialisation);
  `thm:reeh-schlieder-ABL` decoupled from the centralizer hypothesis via the
  App E4 branch. NEW ledger item from this pass: the `prop:tbrme-covariance`
  proof step claiming other $\hat K_k$ terms "commute with $u_t$" was loose
  BEFORE this fix and remains so at the dressed level (centralizer
  membership ⇏ commutation with the Connes cocycle) — `WATCH`, author
  review. Original defect text follows for the record:
  new 2026-07-01 verification swarm; **the one substantive unfixed defect from
  that pass**.) The passage at ~3772–3779 (echoed in the witness model
  ~6111–6124) asserts, citing CLPW, that every type III_λ factor admits
  non-extremal KMS states whose $\mathcal N$-centralizer is a nontrivial
  II$_1$ subfactor, and that CLPW's de Sitter setting "is exactly of this
  form." CLPW prove the opposite for their setting (invariant elements of
  $\mathcal N$ are c-numbers; the nontrivial algebra exists only after
  adjoining the observer, in $\Pi(\mathcal A\rtimes\RR)\Pi$, whose
  projections are NOT in $\mathcal N$). Since the paper insists the record
  projections $\{E_k\}$ live in $\mathcal N$ for physical realisability,
  this is load-bearing for `conj:tomita`'s scope and the witness model's
  non-vacuity. Also: III₁ has no discrete decomposition, and KMS at fixed β
  for a fixed geometric flow is typically unique ⟹ extremal ⟹ trivial
  centralizer. **Fix options:** downgrade to an explicit hypothesis, or
  relocate the $E_k$ into the crossed-product centralizer and weaken the
  physical-records claim. Related minor: DEHK prove II$_1$ only for a
  SINGLE clock bounded below — with ≥2 clocks the algebra is II$_\infty$
  even with all spectra bounded below; a multi-observer extension of
  `thm:gravity-realisation` would silently break the "bounded ⟹ II$_1$"
  slogan (flag in-text if that extension is ever written).
- **`DONE` Support restriction vs Page–Geilker — record-adapted sourcing
  applied.** (A51, resolved 2026-07-02 solver swarm; the deepest fix of the
  set.) The dichotomy {prior source on the past / branch source on the
  future} is replaced by a single non-anticipating source: the semiclassical
  source at τ is the record-conditioned state $\rho_{RC}^\gamma(\tau)$
  (`def:adapted-source`, `eq:rc-source-def`), $\bar{\mathcal F}_\tau$-
  measurable by construction. Conservation: posterior weights frozen between
  records ⇒ fixed convex combination of conserved branch tensors on each
  slab; jumps at record times = Israel–Darmois thin shells at
  $\Sigma_{s_m}$ (the paper's existing junction machinery); distributional
  Bianchi = junction condition. Old `eq:fs-einstein-support` recovered
  EXACTLY as the zero-record sector. Terminal shell now explained: vanishes
  under Ax complete; bounded by horizon-hidden entropy otherwise.
  `pos:geom-nosig` restated in record-relative form (metric exactly as
  informative as the records, no more — proof two lines from adaptedness).
  `prop:page-geilker` SHARPENED: apparatus tracks the realized record
  history, switching at the recorded decay time (which is literally what
  Page–Geilker observed), residual bounded via new `prop:adapted-residual`
  + `fd:thm:quantitative`; worked example rebuilt with explicit epoch-tagged
  records and exact hazard posteriors. Literature anchor: the
  Tilloy–Diósi / KTM / causal-conditional / Oppenheim feedback-gravity
  classification (`rem:adapted-vs-feedback` — also answers the Oppenheim
  decoherence–diffusion engagement item and the Ludescher division-of-labor
  item; future-conditioned sourcing signals per Horowitz–Maldacena /
  Yurtsever–Hockney, which is exactly why the old restriction was the right
  instinct). Costs, honestly hypothesized as (x-a/b/c) in
  `conj:fs-einstein`: uniform contraction across the record tree; summable
  record shells (L² martingale-increment support, a.s. Sobolev control
  OPEN); Born-map fixed point replacing the exact seed-prior identity
  (weakens `thm:tbrme-airtight`(vi) to O(κ)-caveat form). `WATCH` residuals:
  QRF-relativity of the pre-terminal metric (covariant past-light-cone/null-
  shell refinement open); nonlinear-order protocol-independence of the mean
  metric (open in the entire feedback-gravity literature);
  Yurtsever–Hockney cited arXiv-only. Original defect text for the record:
  new 2026-07-01, severity comparable to old A29.) `eq:fs-einstein-support`
  forces $g_k=g_0$ on $J^-(\Sigma_{\tau_f})$ (needed for geometric
  no-signalling), but `prop:page-geilker`(ii) claims any apparatus in
  $M^{\rm ext}$ experiences the branch metric $g_k$. With the
  cosmological-horizon terminal POVM, **every laboratory is in
  $J^-(\Sigma_{\tau_f})$**, so every Cavendish balance sees the prior-sourced
  mean-field metric and the Page–Geilker pathology re-enters. Either introduce
  intermediate-record metric conditioning (risking `pos:geom-nosig`) or scope
  (ii) to $J^+(\Sigma_{\tau_f})$ and confront what sources the observable
  past. Relatedly (same audit): the Oppenheim decoherence–diffusion trade-off
  (Nat. Commun. 14:7910) forbids exactly the deterministic-classical-metric +
  zero-decoherence combination for forward CP hybrids — the two-boundary/
  non-autonomous evasion is plausibly valid but must be *argued* in §10.1;
  and per Ludescher et al. (arXiv:2507.13201) the classical branch metric
  cannot be the BMV mediator, so §13.2's entanglement must be carried entirely
  by the quantum sector — state the division of labor.

## C. Open conjectures in the hypothesis bundle 𝗛

The Master Unification Theorem (`thm:master-unification`) is the **conditional**
implication 𝗛 ⇒ unification; the implication is proved unconditionally, but 𝗛
itself is conjectural. Residual open content:

- **`OPEN` (H0)** Planck-cutoff postulate (Wilsonian EFT scope) — see also §B.
- **`OPEN` (H2)** Semiclassical Einstein coupling + modified Wheeler–DeWitt
  constraint.
- **`OPEN` (H3)** The four speculative closures (clock, Tomita/(CP),
  entropy-smallness, horizon algebra).
- **`OPEN` (H4 residual)** Structural (T)-items not discharged by
  `prop:cutoff-shrinkage` / `prop:coupling-well-defined`.
- **`WATCH` Hypothesis-bundle minimality/completeness.** (A47) Audit 𝗛 for
  circularity, double-counting, and hidden assumptions; confirm "no unlisted
  hypothesis" claim.

## D. Retrocausality / no-go evasions (contested)

- **`WATCH` Wood–Spekkens fine-tuning — operational reading.** (A10) The current
  evasion rebuts the *free-parameter* reading; address the
  causal-structure-independent (operational) definition of fine-tuning.
- **`WATCH` Local Friendliness (2024).** (A10) A 2024 theorem rules out
  fine-tuning-free LF evasion even for nonclassical/cyclic causal models;
  engage it.
- **`WATCH` Shrapnel–Costa obstacle.** (A10) "Causation does not explain
  contextuality" (SEP Fall 2025 flags this as the central threat to retrocausal
  classical ontology); address head-on.
- **`DONE`/NON-THREAT Husain–Javed canonical semiclassical anomaly
  (arXiv:2511.07753, Nov 2025).** (A72, 2026-07-02 Opus) Husain & Javed prove
  the canonical semiclassical Einstein program — replace matter constraint
  terms by expectation values, $H^{\rm eff}=H^G+\langle\hat H^M\rangle(q)$,
  $C_a^{\rm eff}=C_a^G+\langle\hat C_a^M\rangle(q)$ (their (13)–(14)) — has a
  hypersurface-deformation (Dirac) algebra that **fails to close**: EVERY
  bracket picks up an anomaly (their (25)–(27)), e.g.
  $\{H^{\rm eff}(M),H^{\rm eff}(M')\}=C^{\rm eff}(W)-c(W)$, and the diffeo
  bracket $\{C^{\rm eff},C^{\rm eff}\}=C^{\rm eff}([N,N'])+\int g(q)[N,N']^a\beta_a$
  is anomalous too. **EXACT ORIGIN (decisive for us):** the anomaly is NOT a
  variance/fluctuation $\langle\hat T^2\rangle-\langle\hat T\rangle^2$ and NOT
  an off-diagonal coherence term. It is a **state-on-metric functional
  dependence** term: because the matter state (11) is a functional of $q_{ab}$,
  the expectation values $h(x,q)=f(q)\alpha(x)$, $c_a(x,q)=g(q)\beta_a(x)$
  carry $q$-dependence that the Poisson bracket $\{\cdot,\pi^{ab}\}$ hits,
  producing $\{C^G,c\}$, $\{H^G,c\}$ cross-terms with no classical counterpart
  (their (23)–(24)). The anomaly is $O(\langle\hat T\rangle)$ — first order in
  the mean source itself, NOT in its fluctuations. Homogeneous cosmology evades
  it ONLY because $C_a\equiv0$ there (their p.2, citing Husain–Singh 2021), so
  the spatial-derivative brackets vanish identically. No time-gauge fixing
  rescues it; the paper states NO closure-restoring condition exists.
  **WHY THE TBRME FRAMEWORK IS UNAFFECTED (structural, not lucky):**
  (i) The framework does NOT impose a semiclassical *constraint* of
  Husain–Javed form anywhere. Its quantum-gravity constraint is the
  Page–Wootters clock+system operator constraint
  $\hat C_{\alpha,\delta t}=\hat H_c\otimes\id+\id\otimes\hat H_s+\alpha\hat\Sigma$
  (`conj:wdw`, eq:wdw) — a FULLY QUANTUM self-adjoint constraint on
  $\mathcal H_{\rm clock}\otimes\mathcal H_{\rm sys}$, whose algebra is the
  trivial abelian $\{\hat C,\hat C\}=0$, NOT the hypersurface-deformation
  algebra with $\langle\hat T\rangle$ inserted. Husain–Javed's (13)–(14) never
  appear.
  (ii) The framework's *classical* Einstein equation (`conj:fs-einstein`,
  eq:fs-einstein-distrib) is sourced NOT from the full superposed
  $\langle\hat T\rangle$ but from the branch-conditioned $T^{\RC}_{\mu\nu}$
  (`def:adapted-source`, branch postulate (B)) — a definite decohered pointer
  branch. It is per-branch classical GR (`unification.tex` L7477,7624), aligned
  with Halliwell's per-decoherent-branch semiclassical Einstein in
  minisuperspace/WKB (`rem:kent-halliwell`). Husain–Javed's anomaly is a
  statement about the OFF-SHELL constraint *algebra* of a single
  full-superposition source; it does not bear on a per-branch on-shell field
  equation.
  (iii) The Einstein route is Jacobson/Bianchi–Myers operator-algebraic
  Clausius (E0), NOT HKT constraint-closure. HKT was never the framework's
  derivation of Einstein form. So the anomaly cannot block a route the
  framework does not use.
  **HONEST RESIDUE:** (a) Branch-conditioning does NOT "evade" the anomaly by
  *suppressing a fluctuation term* — that reading (the tasking hypothesis) is
  FALSE, because the anomaly is first-order in $\langle\hat T\rangle$, not in
  its variance; decohering off-diagonal coherences leaves $f(q),g(q)$
  untouched. The framework is safe for the different reason (i)–(iii): it never
  writes the offending constraint. (b) The framework's per-branch metric IS
  well-defined enough for a Dirac algebra in the minisuperspace/WKB sector
  (where $C_a\equiv0$, exactly Husain–Javed's own evasion regime); the
  *inhomogeneous* per-branch canonical algebra is NOT claimed to close and is
  NOT needed — Einstein is delivered by the distributional Barrabès–Israel
  fixed point + Clausius, not by canonical closure. (c) So the anomaly does
  NOT reopen an HKT non-circular route: there is nothing to reopen, because the
  framework's Einstein derivation is the (separately circular-flagged) Jacobson
  Clausius identity, not HKT. VERDICT: the 2025 anomaly is a genuine no-go for
  *naive canonical* semiclassical gravity that the framework SIDESTEPS by
  construction (it uses neither the semiclassical constraint nor the
  full-superposition source nor HKT); it is neither a threat nor a new asset
  via the "branching-suppresses-fluctuations" mechanism, which is physically
  incorrect. Add a one-paragraph positioning remark near `rem:kent-halliwell`
  citing Husain–Javed and stating the three structural reasons (i)–(iii).

## E. Temporal-entanglement (Margenau–Hill) witness — `thm:mh-witness` (new)

- **`COND` Macrorealist exclusion is conditional on NIM.** Negativity of
  $M_k=\tfrac1{2p}\{\rho,\Lambda\}$ implies *no joint Kolmogorov law* (Fine /
  Halliwell), but the strong "no classical model / genuine temporal
  entanglement" reading requires the **non-invasive-measurability (NIM /
  adroitness)** hypothesis. The operationally-realized Lüders sequential
  frequencies are a valid nonnegative law a memoryful macrorealist reproduces;
  the signed quasiprobability is never operationally instantiated. This is an
  irreducible interpretational ceiling, not a fixable gap.
- **`COND` Clean iff is projective-only.** $M_k\succeq0\iff[\rho,\Lambda]=0$
  holds for projective $E_k$; for general POVM effects only
  $[\rho,\Lambda]=0\Rightarrow M_k\succeq0$ is unconditional (explicit
  counterexample in the proof).
- **`WATCH` Pin the witness to $M_k$, not $\mathrm{spec}(\varrho_k)$.** The
  Fullwood–Parzygnat state-over-time $\varrho_k$ carries *kinematic* SWAP-type
  negativity independent of $[\rho,\Lambda]$; the meaningful witness is the
  $t_i$-leg marginal $M_k=\mathrm{Tr}_{t_f}\varrho_k$, **not** the spectrum of
  $\varrho_k$. Do not conflate.

  self-consistent achronal ANEC (Graham–Olum / Wall).

  is now orphan-free (275 entries). Swanson consolidated from 3 duplicate keys

  mechanism was false and inverts; C.2 exact fiberwise U5 reduction
  `prop:tdse-effective` + BO α-matching; C.3 Araki first law
  `prop:step6-thermo`; C.4 knitting K1–K5 + `rem:no-wormhole`), with the
  ill-formed Step-4 bifurcation ansatz replaced by the derived two-time
  link state (`sec:temporal-link-derived`: FP state-over-time, dressed-swap

## BOUNDED-ON-BOTH-ENDS + ANCHOR-SCALE RE-GROUNDING (banked 2026-07-03)

Context: user questioned whether "Planck" was the right *lower* bound
("using Planck itself was a guess at best... wouldn't both the upper and
lower bounds have to work off the same physics by nature?"). Lower-bound
research swarm (species-scale lens, one-physics synthesis lens, CKN lens)
concluded: the two bounds DO share one physics at two levels — the
induced-gravity str-sum (E_★²→1/G UV, E_★⁴→Λ IR) and the holographic/
black-hole entropy bound (II₁ trace ceiling + CKN's H² term). "Planck was
a guess" is a misnomer: E_★≈0.65 M_Pl is content-fixed by str[k₁]≈-14.83
(g=GE_★²/ħc⁵≈0.42), its Planckian order OVER-DETERMINED by the anchor, not
chosen. User: "Yes, bank them all in! ABSOLUTELY!"

Three write-ins applied, all four supporting citations verified at primary
source before use (phantom-citation discipline):

1. **Re-grounded `pos:planck-cutoff` (H0).** Retitled "Planck-scale matter
   cutoff" → "Induced-gravity anchor scale E_★ (matter cutoff)" (label kept
   to avoid ripple). New `rem:anchor-overdetermined`: E_★≈0.65 M_Pl is
   str[k₁]-fixed and over-determined (not guessed); "one str-sum, two
   allocations" tie — E_★²→1/G (UV pole), E_★⁴→Λ (IR pole). What H0 still
   posits: EXISTENCE of a sharp band edge; what it no longer needs: its
   Planckian ORDER (field content over-determines it).

2. **`rem:bounded-both-ends`** (after `rem:E1-status`, App. E1). II₁ tracial
   max-entropy state ⇒ ceiling S≤S_dS=A_dS/4G=3π/ΛG = Gibbons–Hawking
   [GibbonsHawking1977, PRD 15 2738 1977] = operator-algebraic Bousso
   N-bound [Bousso2000Nbound, JHEP 0011 038, hep-th/0010252]. UV/IR span
   3π/ΛG ~ (r_dS/ℓ_Pl)² ≈ 10¹²² = the CC hierarchy read as the span between
   two allocations of ONE sum. Honest scope (a)–(c): different bound-types
   (DOF floor vs entropy ceiling; synthesis not derived equivalence);
   existence of E_★ + positive Λ + energy-bounded observer still posited, no
   spectral threshold derived (E1-trace-finiteness(iii)); beyond-bounds
   actively excluded (tracial max-entropy + lem:boltzmann-brain), a limit of
   what is *sayable* not a claim nothing is there.

3. **CKN into `rem:pred-cc-diff`.** Cohen–Kaplan–Nelson [CohenKaplanNelson1999,
   PRL 82 4971, hep-th/9803132] as 2nd black-hole derivation of the H²
   CC term (root of the HDE papers already cited). "Which cutoff is which":
   CKN's IR-locked VACUUM-ENERGY cutoff ~10⁻³ eV ≠ E_★'s Planckian DOF/anchor
   cutoff — conflating them collapses g by ~60 orders. Framework's thermal-
   time route AVOIDS the Hsu w=0 defect [Hsu2004, PLB 594 13, hep-th/0403052]:
   H² term is a thermal-time RG fixed point, not a saturated IR-cutoff budget,
   so w set by running-vacuum structure not pinned to 0.

Citations verified (WebFetch/WebSearch, 2026-07-03): GibbonsHawking1977,
Bousso2000Nbound, CohenKaplanNelson1999, Hsu2004 — all authors/journal/year
confirmed at arXiv/APS. Build: latexmk EXIT=0, 227 pp. (was 226), 4 new
labels resolved, zero undefined refs/cites, no multiply-defined warnings.

## E_★–g–Λ OVER-DETERMINATION: LIVE-OR-TAUTOLOGY SWARM → **TAUTOLOGY** (2026-07-03)

Question banked from the two-pole lens: is "both gravitational poles pinned
by the ONE Type-II additive constant" a LIVE falsifiable consistency relation
(fixing G forces a measurable Λ/ν) or a Sakharov-bookkeeping TAUTOLOGY?

**5-agent workflow (4 attack lenses + high-effort adjudicator), all sources
primary-verified. VERDICT: TAUTOLOGY, high confidence, UNANIMOUS.**

Decisive chain (primary-verified):
1. Visser gr-qc/0204062 Eqs 20–23: induced action has FOUR independent bare
   constants; Λ₀ and G₀ SEPARATE. a0 (quartic→Λ, str[k0]) and a1 (quadratic
   →1/G, str[k1]) are algebraically distinct supertraces feeding INDEPENDENT
   counterterms. Any Λ absorbable at fixed G. Visser verbatim: "the smallness
   of the observed cosmological constant is very much put in by hand."
2. CLPW 2206.10780: the ONE Type-II additive constant is STATE-INDEPENDENT,
   lands only on A/4G_N (Newton pole), cancels in every entropy DIFFERENCE →
   cannot enter any EoM/first law/QFC → cannot fix Λ dynamically. Relabels,
   doesn't constrain.
3. Moreno-Pulido & Solà 2005.03164 (EPJC 80 692 2020): RVM running of 1/G lives
   in the a1 sector only. [CORRECTED by the later g↔ν swarm — the structure is
   NOT ν∝m²/M_Pl²; it is Eq 7.3: ν_eff = (1/2π)(1/6−ξ)(M_X²/M_Pl²)[1+(m²/M_X²)ln],
   M_X = mass of the HEAVIEST CONTRIBUTING field (lines 1078/1141: SM particles
   contribute negligibly since m²/M_X²≪1). Conclusion unchanged (SM ν negligible),
   functional form corrected. See the g↔ν DEAD-END entry below.]
4. Only horn that could resurrect liveness = STRICT Sakharov (Λ₀=1/G₀=0):
   self-destructs — (a) extra postulate NOT delivered by Type-II; (b) even
   granted, predicts Λ~E_★⁴~10⁻² M_Pl⁴, the 120-order induced-CC catastrophe;
   escaping needs independent Λ₀ → back to tautology.

**Consequence for "G = where we are in the span" (user's reframe):** lands on
the SELECTION branch, not derivation. G = 3π/ΛN is a RELABEL (N a free
boundary condition), so G is our contingent/underivable "address" — vindicates
the "unprovable parallel realities" intuition, kills the derivation version.

**CORRECTION APPLIED (overclaim I introduced 2026-07-03 in rem:bounded-both-ends
+ rem:anchor-overdetermined, now walked back):**
- rem:bounded-both-ends: removed "E_★ FIXES the IR ceiling" and "hierarchy is
  the span... rather than an unexplained tuning". Rewrote: str[k0]≠str[k1] feed
  INDEPENDENT counterterms Λ₀,G₀ [VisserInduced2002, Solodukhin2011]; Type-II
  constant is state-independent, lands on A/4G, can't fix Λ [CLPW2022]; poles do
  NOT cross-check; eq:UVIR-span is a RELABELING not a derivation. Added
  eq:G-as-address G=3π/ΛN recasting G as dimensionless address N (Dirac
  large-number question SHARPENED not resolved; N a free boundary condition).
- rem:anchor-overdetermined: retitled "over-determined"→"fixed by the field
  content"; softened "over-determines to be Planckian"→"fixes to be Planckian";
  fixed "quartic part feeds counterterm AND HENCE Λ"→"is what the counterterm
  absorbs... two LOGICALLY INDEPENDENT counterterms, bookkeeping not cross-check".
- prop:pred-cc / g=(E_★/E_Pl)² UNCHANGED and still honest: it predicts absence
  of the E_Pl⁴ catastrophe (counterterm absorbs vacuum energy independently) —
  which is exactly scoping option (A) the adjudicator endorsed. NOT presented as
  a G-Λ cross-check.

Build: latexmk EXIT=0, 227 pp., eq:G-as-address resolves, zero undefined
refs/cites. The two-pole "new prediction" hunt is CLOSED: no live relation

## g↔ν RUNNING-VACUUM DOOR: SCOPING SWARM → **DEAD-END** (2026-07-03)

Adjudicator PRIMARY-VERIFIED the decisive point itself (pdftotext of
Moreno-Pulido & Solà 2005.03164). Question: is a heavy-field g↔ν link a door
worth opening for a live DESI-testable prediction? VERDICT: DEAD-END, high conf.

Decisive: Lens A's "worth opening" (identify M_X=E_star, reuse SM str[k1], add
no field) MISREADS the source. Eq 7.3: ν_eff=(1/2π)(1/6−ξ)(M_X²/M_P²)[1+(m²/M_X²)ln];
line 1078 defines M_X as a PHYSICAL GUT scale (~10^16 GeV), line 1141: "SM
particles make no significant contribution (m²/M_X²≪1); only particles near the
GUT scale contribute." So M_X and contributing-field-mass are LOCKED — you
cannot have the large M_X² prefactor AND untouched SM str[k1].

Three costs converge (each closes an escape):
1. ONE COUNTERTERM TWO KNOBS: ν and g are the SAME a1 supertrace (Eq 6.4 ≡ Eq 7.3
   share (ξ−1/6)·mass² functional) — moving ν moves g. Same structural reason
   as the two-pole TAUTOLOGY.
2. NATURAL SCALE OVERSHOOTS: field at E_star=0.65 M_Pl gives ν~1.1e-2 (ξ=0,
   recomputed) — ~10x above loose ν~1e-3, ~40x above the Type-II Ġ/G ceiling
   |ν|≲(2-5)e-4. EXCLUDED.
3. WINDOW NEEDS A FOREIGN TUNED SCALE: ν~1e-3 needs M_X~0.19 M_Pl — an
   intermediate scale that is neither GUT (10^16→ν~1e-8) nor E_star; framework
   lacks it, and any sub-cutoff field re-shifts str[k1]=-14.83 (SM-content
   spin-weighted supertrace, unification.tex ~line 6685). Sign also wrong at
   face value (observed ν>0, str[k1]<0).
Note: Antipin-Melic non-decoupling (leading running ∝ heavy m²) does NOT rescue
— it is exactly what makes a near-Planckian field OVERSHOOT ν. Every route closes.

Cost-benefit strongly negative: even a positive DESI ν is NON-DIAGNOSTIC
(degenerate across RVM/HDE/quintessence); DESI DR2's 3.1σ is a w0-wa CPL result
that does NOT translate to RVM ν (RVM keeps w=-1); the ~4σ RVM signal is in the
Type-I matter-decay branch, NOT the G-running Type-II branch the framework
inhabits. Recommendation: LEAVE DOOR CLOSED.

WRITE-IN (recommendation items 2+3, high-confidence honesty addition): added a
foreclosing caveat paragraph to rem:pred-cc-diff — states the falsifiable-but-
FALSIFIED native prediction (ν~1e-2 from a field at E_star, excluded >1 order),
explains no intermediate scale exists to lift ν without de-tuning g=0.42, and
frames the framework's actual content as a CONSISTENCY REQUIREMENT (ν
unobservably small in the Type-II G-running branch, passed by null fits) — NOT a
live ν prediction. Falsifiable content of prop:pred-cc = the structural
"H² running with E_Pl⁴ term absent", not a number for ν. New bibitem
MorenoPulidoSola2020 (EPJC 80 692 2020, arXiv:2005.03164) — metadata
WebFetch-verified; Eq 7.3 pdftotext-verified by the adjudicator.

## QNEC-SATURATION GAP SURVEY → **NARROWED** (2026-07-03)

The genuine open problem (Axis 2): framework HAS monotonicity (inequality
δ²S_rel≥0), Einstein needs SATURATION (equality). 6-agent survey (5 lenses +
adjudicator; lens E "optimistic steelman" died on StructuredOutput cap, verdict
rests on 4/5 + adjudicator's own primary re-verification). VERDICT: NARROWED,
high confidence.

PROVEN (theorem/argued-grade, primary-verified):
- Monotonicity/QNEC INEQUALITY: general, non-perturbative, interacting QFT
  (Ceyhan-Faulkner 1812.04683; new proof 2503.04651; crossed-product KFLRSS
  2312.07646). = what framework already has.
- SATURATION equality proven only in narrow classes: holographic large-N in
  every state (LLS 1802.02584); interacting CFTs WITH A TWIST GAP (Balakrishnan
  et al 1906.08274 — "QNEC always saturated for CFTs w/ twist gap"); near-vacuum
  modular limit (Ceyhan-Faulkner). PROVABLY FAILS for free fields (LLS contact
  term). CONJECTURED for generic interacting theories.
- No Type-II/crossed-product result delivers the equality — all give the
  inequality or ASSUME the area law (Faulkner-Speranza 2405.00847; Dorau-Much
  2510.24491 assume A/4G).

KEY PHYSICS POINT (adjudicator primary-RE-VERIFIED, resolving a lens split):
CGM danger window is (d-2)/2 < Δ < d/2 = 1 < Δ < 2 (d=4). Higgs |H|² sits at

γ-log washout (1+2γ log Mℓ) is qualitative/uncontrolled, EXPECTED not proven.

## ROUTE (c) — CAN TYPE-II STRUCTURE DERIVE SATURATION? → NEW NO-GO (2026-07-03)

Question: can the framework's OWN crossed-product/thermal-time machinery upgrade
QNEC MONOTONICITY (inequality, which it has) to SATURATION (equality, which
Einstein needs) WITHOUT importing A/4G? 6-agent swarm (5 lenses construct+refute,
adjudicator, all decisive citations primary-verified). VERDICT:
VIABLE-ONLY-WITH-INJECTED-INPUT = effectively a CLEAN NO-GO, high confidence
(4 lenses "foreclosed", 1 "viable-only-with-input" — same verdict, honest framing
names the escape).

**NEW RESULT — crossed-product/thermal-time saturation no-go (now
prop:crossed-product-saturation-nogo + rem:saturation-residual-localised, ~line
18036, 229 pp.):**
Type-II crossed product gives S_gen = <βq> + S_rel + c0 (CLPW additive form). The
trace adjoins an INDEPENDENT clock/area sector <βq> and leaves S_rel — hence
∂²_v S_rel and the saturation defect D = δ²S_rel − E_can ≥ 0 — UNTOUCHED, equal to
the bare Type-III value. So the trace/weight/tracial-state/thermal-time/
two-boundary-seed CANNOT constrain D. Free-field WITNESS (decisive modus tollens):
the whole construction exists for free fields (free wedge = Type III₁ by
Bisognano-Wichmann/Araki; CLPW build it on free graviton+matter), where D ~
<(∂_vφ)²> ≠ 0 is PROVEN (LLS 1802.02584). Any "structure ⇒ D=0" would force
free-field saturation → contradiction. QED. Monotonicity D≥0 is STRUCTURAL (HSMI
positivity); saturation D=0 is DYNAMICAL.

**Sharp localisation (the value):** framework OWNS (a) the inequality D≥0 and (b)
the area/clock sector <βq>. The SINGLE missing ingredient is a TWIST GAP on the
seed's correlation data (absence of non-identity light-ray ops of twist τ≤d−2
besides T_μν; = anomalous-dim washout of the minimal-twist higher-spin tower whose
replica contact term is the <(∂_vφ)²> obstruction; BCFLSM 1906.08274). This input
is interaction-dependent, provably NOT derivable from any framework postulate, and
adopting it IS the BCFLSM twist-gap theorem → route (c) collapses into (a)/(b)
(provably; twist gap = exactly BCFLSM's hypothesis). The framework-internal
alternative (impose <(∂_vφ)²>=0 via final boundary condition) is CIRCULAR
(hand-picks a saturating config) AND insufficient (Einstein needs D=0 in EVERY
state; state-selection = measure-zero). Honest residual: saturation is a SPECTRAL
(twist-gap) property of the two-boundary seed's correlators, whose justification
within TBRME is THE genuine open problem.

Caveat written in (residual uncertainty #2): the "two-boundary injects only into
<βq>" step presumes the boundary condition selects a STATE not the operator
SPECTRUM; if a future axiom restricts the seed spectrum it would be SUPPLYING the
twist gap by fiat and must be argued as such — flagged explicitly in the remark.

Frontier scan (Lens E, primary-verified): nobody derives the equality
structurally; every Einstein/equality result imports A/4G (Dorau-Much 2510.24491
= first-order + free-field + ASSUMED Bekenstein-Hawking, never reaches 2nd
variation) or a background-boost area (Cao-Faulkner-Wang). Consistent with the
no-go.

Build: latexmk EXIT=0, 229 pp., prop:crossed-product-saturation-nogo +
rem:saturation-residual-localised resolve, no undefined refs/cites.

## TWO-BOUNDARY SPECTRUM PROBE → **STATE-ONLY** (no-go assumption DISCHARGED) (2026-07-03)

The one open door: does the TBRME two-boundary (retrocausal) condition RESTRICT
the seed operator SPECTRUM (supplying the twist gap → closing the saturation
no-go) or only SELECT A STATE (no-go stands, residual open)? 6-agent swarm
(5 lenses incl. one that READ the axioms + one retrocausal-light-ray long shot).
VERDICT: STATE-ONLY, high confidence, UNANIMOUS 5/5 lenses.
[NOTE: adjudicator's structured prose fields returned as "test" placeholder stubs
— a glitch; verdict enum STATE-ONLY/high is valid and the 5 lenses are unanimous,
thorough, primary-verified, so the conclusion is robust independent of the
adjudicator prose. Writein synthesized from the lens findings directly.]

Decisive reasoning (unanimous):
- Lens B (READ ax:data L571, ax:microcausality L577, conj:wdw L3610): the
  two-boundary axioms are MATTER-AGNOSTIC — specify ρ_i + terminal effects
  {E_k}⊂N, reference NO twist/OPE/operator-dimension data. A terminal effect
  E_k∈N reweights outcomes on the FIXED algebra N; it cannot delete operators
  from N. So they act identically on free and interacting nets.
- Free-field parity test (all 5 lenses): a free-field TBRME carries the IDENTICAL
  two-boundary data yet D~<(∂_vφ)²>≠0 is PROVEN (LLS 1802.02584). Were the
  two-boundary condition to supply a twist gap it would equally (falsely) supply
  it for free matter where none exists → modus tollens. Converts "selects a state"
  from ASSUMPTION to THEOREM.
- Lens C (retrocausal light-ray long shot): even if a final effect E_k could zero
  ∫<(∂_vφ)²> on the cut, <(∂_vφ)²> is a POSITIVE operator whose null integral
  vanishes ONLY in the vacuum-on-cut config = measure-zero state-selection, NOT
  spectrum-restriction, and Einstein needs D=0 in EVERY state. Fails both hurdles
  (universality + free-field). The long shot closed cleanly.

CONSEQUENCE: the no-go's single stated assumption is DISCHARGED. Upgraded the
caveat in rem:saturation-residual-localised from "presumes it selects a state..."
to a discharged theorem: matter-agnostic axioms + E_k∈N can't delete operators +
free-field parity ⇒ the two-boundary condition provably selects a state; the only
framework-internal route to D=0 (impose <(∂_vφ)²>=0 via terminal effect) is
measure-zero, non-universal. prop:crossed-product-saturation-nogo is now a fully
clean result with NO open assumption: no property of the crossed product, thermal
time, OR the retrocausal two-boundary condition supplies saturation. The twist gap
remains the un-derived spectral input = THE genuine open problem.

Build: latexmk EXIT=0, 229 pp., no undefined refs/cites.

NET: the user's "good feeling" chased the right question; the answer strengthened

## SWARM #1: EFFECTIVE-REGIME SATURATION DISCHARGE → STILL-OPEN (but splits + sharpens; 1 real correction) (2026-07-03)

Question: does GR need EXACT saturation D=0, or only EFFECTIVE (macroscopic/leading-order)
saturation, and is the latter provable for the SM? 6-agent swarm. VERDICT: STILL-OPEN,
high confidence — but genuinely productive (a clean split + a sharpened residual + a real
physics correction to my own no-go write-in).

THE SPLIT (banked as rem:effective-saturation-regime):
- LINEARIZED Einstein (n=1) CLOSES in-regime — and never needed saturation. It rests on
  first-order stationarity δS_rel=0 (first law, a THEOREM: thm:E0-lin + cor:E0-lin-SM). The
  CGM danger term is suppressed by (μ_O/E_star)^{2Δ} (Higgs escapes Δ=2+γ>d/2) and
  prop:conformal-cutoff reproduces the first law to O((E/E_star)^α). Genuinely discharged.
- NONLINEAR Einstein (n=2, the framework's actual route) STILL needs the twist gap. Decisive
  Q4: the defect enters the smeared 2nd variation at ℓ^{d-2}, the SAME power as 2π<T_vv>, so
  D/<T_vv>=O(1) at EVERY wavelength — carries no power of R, NOT suppressed by large R. The
  relaxation weakens "exact D=0 in every state" only to the parametric bound
  D/<T_vv> ≲ (μ_O R)^{2γ} at R~1/E_star, holding IFF the SM light-ray spectrum has γ>0
  (positive minimal-twist anomalous dim) over the operating range = the same twist gap
  prop:crossed-product-saturation-nogo shows is not framework-derivable. Higgs γ>0 escape
  genuine but fragile (Wilson-Fisher can push Δ_{|H|²} back below d/2).

REAL CORRECTION (verified by me at primary source, ar5iv LLS 1802.02584 App B — swarm was
RIGHT this time, unlike its LHAASO finding): the free-field defect is <∂_v φ>² (SQUARE OF
THE ONE-POINT FUNCTION = coherent classical amplitude, "quadratic in expectation values"),
NOT <(∂_v φ)²> (fluctuation/contact). My no-go write-in had <(∂_v φ)²> at 5 loci (18103,
18129, 18140, 18167, 18170). This STRENGTHENS the no-go: <∂_v φ>² is a CLASSICAL MACROSCOPIC
amplitude, largest in the coherent slowly-varying states the framework uses, and SURVIVES
coarse-graining — so the defect is manifestly not a UV artifact evadable by going macroscopic.
FIXED all 5 loci + reframed the "positive operator null-integral" clause to the mean-field-free
(measure-zero) configuration. [Discipline note: verified the correction at primary source before
applying — the swarm flagged its OWN uncertainty (#3), and its prior LHAASO finding was wrong,
so trust-but-verify held.]

NET on the gravity arm: honest position sharpens further — the LINEAR gravitational response is
now a stated THEOREM in-regime; the NONLINEAR response is postulated with its residual pinned to
