## ONE-LINE VERDICT
The analog transfer of sigma^(2) is **NOT a measurable prediction and NOT an honest
consistency check**: (K-i) the IDENTITY KILL is DECISIVE-BUT-INVERTED — sigma^(2) is
provably NOT Beliaev-Landau (it is the nu=0 DIAGONAL/reactive half of the Kubo-Mori
metric; Beliaev-Landau is the nu!=0 OFF-DIAGONAL on-shell Im Sigma; overlap=0), so the
"framework rediscovers Beliaev damping" consistency claim is FALSE and, worse, sigma^(2)
carries NO dissipative/linewidth channel for an experiment to read; (K-ii) the WINDOW
KILL is PARTIAL — the hydrodynamic and nbar^2-at-O(1) bands DO overlap but only in the
deep-IR corner hbar Omega <~ kB T_H << mu (relative width T_H/mu ~ 0.05), so nbar~O(1)
only for the few lowest horizon modes, not O(1)-everywhere; (K-iii) the DISPERSION KILL
is SPLIT — dispersion does NOT break the razor algebraically (S_prod/W_diag=1.000000
holds for dispersive BdG energies), but it DOES supply a candidate delta_t = xi/c ~ 2.3
ms (kinematic choice, not a derivation). **GRADE: SPECULATION (unchanged from run-25),
with the kill LOCATED: the barrier is not occupation (analog fixes it for low modes) but
the ELASTIC/DIAGONAL character of sigma^(2).** g_N=GM/R^2 untouched. ZERO writes to
unification.tex; ZERO writes to ISSUES.md.

## THE OBJECT (inherited BINDING, runs 22-25)
- S_prod^(2) = (beta^2/2) Var_p0(K_aa), K_aa = <a|:phi^4:|a> free-Fock-diagonal, beta=2pi.
- RAZOR (run-25): S_prod/W_diag = 1.000000 at every N — production = EXACTLY the nu=0
  number-diagonal (elastic/forward/zero-frequency) Kubo-Mori weight; off-diagonal
  (nu!=0) weight = 38.55 (99.97% of the metric) contributes ZERO. REPRODUCED from
  scratch (analog_refute.py PART 0): S_prod=0.009987, W_off=38.549467, kappa=454 (ledger
  453.1, matches to discretization).

## THE THREE KILLS (each COMPUTED, analog_refute.py + .tex)

### (K-i) IDENTITY KILL — DECISIVE, INVERTED. [the load-bearing kill]
- Beliaev-Landau damping = Gamma(k) = -Im Sigma(k, omega=E_k)/hbar, a Fermi-golden-rule
  ON-SHELL rate: gamma = (pi/hbar^2) Sum |A_ik|^2 delta(omega_ik-Omega)(f_i-f_k).
  The delta(omega_ik-Omega) makes it ENERGY-TRANSFERRING (dN=+/-1) = OFF-DIAGONAL (nu!=0).
  Landau (3D): Gamma_L ~ T^4; Beliaev (T=0): Gamma_B ~ k^5 (spontaneous 1->2 decay).
- The framework's razor puts ALL production in the nu=0 DIAGONAL weight.
  => sigma^(2) and Beliaev-Landau are the TWO ORTHOGONAL HALVES of one Kubo-Mori metric.
  NUMERICAL WITNESS (PART 1): the energy-transferring monomials (a^d4 + a^4) have
  identically ZERO diagonal => contribute 0 to S_prod; production is carried by the
  forward dN=0 (Sum n^2) structure.
- CONSEQUENCE: "framework rediscovers Beliaev-Landau from modular theory" is FALSE
  (the razor EXCLUDES that sector). And a linewidth is an off-diagonal (Im Sigma)
  observable — sigma^(2) lives in the diagonal, so the "excess dissipative signature"
  has NO dissipative channel.
- SELF-ADVERSARIAL DEFENCE (PART 6): the fluctuation-dissipation rescue (static nu=0
  response = w->0 dissipative response) FAILS — W_diag is the ISOTHERMAL (reactive)
  susceptibility (a thermodynamic 2nd derivative, no Im part); dissipation is the small-w
  OFF-diagonal weight, which the razor assigns zero production even in the continuum.

### (K-ii) WINDOW KILL — PARTIAL (not clean).
- Steinhauer 87Rb sonic horizon: mu/kB~8 nK, T_H=0.43 nK, xi=2 um => c~0.88 mm/s,
  T_H/mu~0.05, n0 xi^3 ~ 172 atoms/healing-volume.
- nbar(Omega)=[exp(hbar Omega/kB T_H)-1]^-1. Hydrodynamic window k xi < 1. nbar>~1 needs
  k xi <~ 0.02 (hbar Omega <~ kB T_H). At k xi=0.5 nbar~0.02; at k xi~1 (healing scale,
  where a rate peaks) nbar~1e-4.
- The nbar^2-at-O(1) band and the hydrodynamic window OVERLAP, but only in the deep-IR
  corner (relative width T_H/mu ~ 0.05). NOT the clean "windows disjoint" kill (they
  overlap), NOT the massive-matter catastrophe (nbar~1e-168): the analog genuinely
  inverts the occupation for the coldest modes. But O(1) only for the lowest few.

### (K-iii) DISPERSION KILL — SPLIT.
- (3b) Dispersion does NOT break the razor: S_prod=nu=0-diagonal is a Kubo-Mori
  IDENTITY; verified to hold with dispersive BdG energies E(k)=hbar c k sqrt(1+(k xi/2)^2)
  (S_prod/W_diag=1.000000 for k xi in {0.1,1,3}). Only the DERIVATION from boost-KMS is
  voided by dispersion; the CONCLUSION (production=diagonal) survives.
- (3a) Dispersion DOES supply a candidate delta_t = xi/c ~ 2.3 ms (healing-crossing
  time) — the finite relaxation the abstract type-III_1 single mode provably lacked
  (run-25 finite-size beat, no Ruelle time). BUT it is a KINEMATIC choice, not a
  derivation; the run-25 nbar^2-vs-nbar^4 fork survives (modular clock -> nbar^2;
  collision clock 1/Gamma_coll -> nbar^4).

## MEASURABILITY ARITHMETIC (PART 4, experimentalist; #37-home-(c) done with units)
- Real phonon dissipation at nK: Beliaev (T-independent) Gamma_B/Omega=(3/640 pi)(k xi)^4

## ONE-LINE VERDICT
Under the acoustic (Unruh 1981) map, sigma^(2) = W_diag = the nu=0 REACTIVE (isothermal)
Kubo-Mori susceptibility, ORTHOGONAL to the dissipative Beliaev-Landau linewidth
(nu!=0, on-shell Im Sigma; overlap 0). So sigma^(2) is NOT Beliaev-Landau and carries NO
measurable dissipative channel. **GRADE: SPECULATION (unchanged from run 25), barrier
LOCATED = the reactive/elastic CHARACTER of sigma^(2).** Refuter (i) DECISIVE-BUT-INVERTED;
(iii) SPLIT; delta_t NEGATIVE (weaker than abstract).

## CROSS-ARM RECONCILIATION (important)
The concurrent R-refuter arm reached the INVERTED-identity conclusion first (its
`ledger_run30.md` K-i). My A1-map first pass INITIALLY claimed the opposite ("identity
FIRES, sigma^(2) IS Landau-forward entropy production"). ON SCRUTINY THE R-REFUTER IS
CORRECT and I RETRACT my first pass. I independently reproduced the decisive step:
- S_prod/W_diag=1 with DISPERSIVE Bogoliubov energies E(k)=hbar c k sqrt(1+(kxi/2)^2)
  for kxi in {0.1,0.5,1,3}: ratio = 1.000000 exactly (razor is an algebraic identity).
- The energy-transferring monomials (a^d4+a^4) have ZERO number-diagonal => 0 production;
  sigma^(2) is blind to the damping channel. Reactive-vs-dissipative split, overlap 0.
The two arms now AGREE on the load-bearing physics. My arm ADDS three complementary,
non-contradictory pieces (below).

## THE THREE COMMISSIONED QUESTIONS — ANSWERED (per-step, each with window)

### PER-STEP WINDOW AUDIT
| Ingredient | Transfer + window | Grade |
|---|---|---|
| KMS reference state | analog-Hawking KMS, kxi<<1; measured (de Nova 2019) | PLAUSIBLE (in window) |
| Modular/boost gen | acoustic Killing flow, kxi<<1; breaks at healing scale | PLAUSIBLE-in-window / NEG at xi |
| Interacting vertex | maps to Bogoliubov **CUBIC** (not phi^4); O(g^2)=Beliaev-Landau | MAPS EXACTLY (to BL) |
| Modular first law + S_rel^(2) | operator-algebra THEOREM; transfers on window | THEOREM-in-model |
| Razor (nu=0) under dispersion | conclusion S_prod/W_diag=1 ROBUST; boost-KMS derivation voided | refuter (iii) SPLIT |
| delta_t from healing length | tau_xi=hbar/mu = CORRELATION time (regulates continuum) NOT relax | NEGATIVE (weaker than abstract) |
| Beliaev-Landau identity | sigma^(2)=nu=0 REACTIVE half; Gamma_BL=nu!=0 DISSIPATIVE half; overlap 0 | refuter (i) INVERTED |

### (1) DISPERSION-vs-RAZOR (refuter iii) — SPLIT
- (3b) razor's algebraic conclusion DISPERSION-ROBUST: S_prod/W_diag=1.000000 for
  dispersive BdG energies kxi in {0.1,0.5,1,3} (independently verified; matches R-refuter).
  It is an identity (S_prod := number-diagonal variance = W_diag).
- (3a) only the boost-KMS DERIVATION is voided by dispersion (kxi>~1: no boost). Decisive
  ratio k_T*xi = kB T/mu: de Nova 2019 = 0.40, Steinhauer 2016 = 2.87 — a LARGE Landau
  rate (kB T>~mu) lives at the healing scale where boost-KMS is gone. So the strong form of
  refuter (iii) FAILS (razor survives algebraically) but the derivation-justification dies.

### (2) delta_t-FROM-HEALING-LENGTH — NEGATIVE (weaker than abstract)
tau_xi = xi/c_s = **hbar/mu** = CORRELATION time (inverse chemical potential; UV cutoff on
the modular autocorrelation C(s)). REAL GAIN: it supplies the continuum regulator the
abstract analysis lacked (run 25: spectral density "decays and stays dead" now has a
physical cutoff omega_xi~mu/hbar; C(s) decays instead of the finite-size beat). BUT the
sigma formula needs the RELAXATION time = 1/Gamma_Landau, and Gamma_L*tau_xi << 1
(diluteness sqrt(n0 a^3)<<1). delta_t_relax = 1/Gamma_L >> tau_xi. Plugging tau_xi
overcounts by 1/(Gamma_L tau_xi)>>1. So dispersion supplies A physical time (correlation)
but NOT the sigma-formula relaxation time. (Agrees with R-refuter: "kinematic, not derived";
mine specifies WHICH time = correlation ħ/μ, and WHY it's wrong = it's not the relaxation.)

### (3) BELIAEV-LANDAU COMPARISON — COMPUTED (refuter i INVERTED)
Canonical rates, coefficients CONFIRMED from literature:
- Beliaev (T=0): Gamma_B = 3 hbar k^5/(640 pi m n0); exact dimensionless Gamma_B/omega =
  (3/160)(a_s/xi)(kxi)^4. [Derezinski-Li-Napiorkowski 2024 Thm 6: Im Sigma_k=c k^5,
  c=3/(640pi rho0) — extracted from the PDF via pdftotext.]
- Landau (kB T>~mu): Gamma_L = (3 pi/8) a_s (kB T/hbar) k. [Pitaevskii-Stringari cond-mat/9708104.]
- Framework vertex = Bogoliubov CUBIC (NOT a fundamental phi^4); its two O(g^2) outputs
  ARE Beliaev + Landau. This identifies WHICH physical object sits on the dissipative side.
- IDENTITY VERDICT: sigma^(2)=W_diag is the nu=0 REACTIVE (isothermal, real, dN=0) half;
  Gamma_BL is the nu!=0 DISSIPATIVE (on-shell Im Sigma, dN=+-1) half; overlap=0. A forward
  process does not damp. So sigma^(2) is NOT Beliaev-Landau, and carries NO linewidth.
  Occupation bookkeeping now EXCLUDES (not matches): Beliaev nbar^0/nbar^1, Landau linewidth
  nbar^1; sigma^(2) razor nbar^2 reactive-diagonal. Refuter (i) DECISIVE-BUT-INVERTED.
- RETRACTION: my first-pass "nu=0 nbar^2 = Landau-forward entropy production; identity FIRES"
  over-read the reactive diagonal as a forward-damping channel. Corrected throughout .tex/.py.

## RATES FOR REALISTIC BEC (de Nova/Steinhauer, probe kxi=0.5; units+window)
| set | c_s | xi | mu(nK) | kB T/mu | Gamma_L (1/s) | lifetime | Gamma_B (1/s) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## ARM C — THE meV^4 BINARY CALCULATION (Edit-E gate)
Staged: `scratchpad/g31_mev4.tex` (4pp, latex EXIT=0, 0 undefined refs) + `g31_mev4.py` (runs clean).

### THE GATE (ISSUES l.3532-3545): binary by design
Is `rho_Lambda^{1/4} ~ meV ~ m_lightest` the framework's meV^4 EQUATION
(=> m_lightest + measured Lambda => a G-INDEPENDENT second relation for N),
or NUMEROLOGY (=> Edit E dies, Avenue-1 footnote closed NO)?

### VERDICT: **NUMEROLOGY. Edit E CLOSED = NO.** (THEOREM-grade, four independent refutations)

The coincidence is a THEOREM only if a framework mechanism forces
`rho_Lambda = C m_lightest^4` (C=O(1)), equivalently the second relation
`N = (3pi/C)(M_Pl/m_lightest)^4 x [G-carrying factors]`. This candidate
equation is refuted FOUR ways, EACH the framework's OWN standing result or
OWN cited source:

- **(R-a) WRONG DIMENSIONAL TYPE.** Framework reads Lambda := 3/r_dS^2 as a
  CURVATURE (gap^2) -> 1e-122 [ISSUES l.2879-2882, Bankable Positive #1].
  Energy-DENSITY reading is gap^4 -> 1e-244, wrong by 122 orders. Both
  rho_L^{1/4} and m_lightest^4 are energy-DENSITY objects; Lambda in the
  framework is a curvature. Coincidence pairs objects the framework does
  NOT identify with Lambda. Wrong target-shape from the outset.
- **(R-b) prop:pred-cc (tex l.13419-13458).** Planckian rho_vac ~M_Pl^4 is
  absorbed by the SAKHAROV counterterm, NOT anything spectrum-level. Type-II
  trace fixes an ENTROPY zero-point (shift on A/4G, cancels in every entropy
  difference, "cannot absorb an energy density"). Running is Lambda_eff =
  Lambda_0 + c_Lambda H0^2 + O(H0^2 (H0/M_Pl)^2) -> H^2, never m^4. No channel
  makes a matter mass threshold set rho_vac.
- **(R-c) Sola RVM — the framework's OWN cited source — is m^4-FREE. VERBATIM**
  (Sola_RVM.txt l.3375, l.5957): renormalized VED and vacuum pressure "free
  from quartic mass dependencies" / "free of the undesired quartic mass
  contributions ~m^4 from any quantum matter field"; "if they were present ...
  would recreate the need for extreme fine-tuning ... reproduce such an ugly
  feature of the cosmological constant problem." Retained running nu_eff ∝
  M_X^2/M_Pl^2, M_X = HEAVIEST contributing field (Eq 7.3) => rho_vac dominated
  by HEAVIEST, never LIGHTEST. A neutrino m^4 term is exactly what the source
  renormalizes AWAY.
- **(R-d) No spectrum-level subtraction available.** Only framework-internal
  route to "pick the last/lightest threshold" is a terminal effect;
  rem:saturation-residual-localised (tex l.18874-18933) proves terminal
  effects select a STATE, "cannot delete operators," reach measure-zero
  (free-field parity test). Plus SIGN: fermionic last threshold is WRONG sign
  for rho_vac [Shapiro-Sola hep-ph/9910462].

### NUMBERS (all in g31_mev4.py; every input provenance-tagged)
- rho_L^{1/4}=2.31 meV; sqrt(dm21^2)=8.66 meV; sqrt(|dm31^2|)=50.0 meV;
  Sum(m_nu) pred 59-71 meV. **Order-of-magnitude coincidence, NOT equality**:
  no O(1) makes 2.31=8.66=50. Factor 3.75 (solar) to 21.6 (atm).
- (M_Pl/2.31meV)^4 = 1e122.9; target N=3pi/Lambda_pl = 1e122.4. The bare
  quartic "works" ONLY at m~2.3 meV = rho_L^{1/4} ITSELF (circular: reproduces
  rho_L because rho_L was fed in), MISSES the actual neutrino scale by 1.9-3.4
  decades. Tuning C to close the gap = the retracted fake-G O(1e6)-factor
  pathology. REFUSED.

### CONDITIONAL POSITIVE BRANCH (had it been positive): TRICHOTOMY = (T2)
Force rho_Lambda=C m^4 through Lambda~G rho_Lambda and G=3pi/(Lambda N):
`N = 3pi/(G^2 C m^4)`. **G on BOTH sides** (Lambda imported G; density rho_L
imported G via rho_crit=3H0^2 M_Pl_red^2). Eliminating G needs an independent
Lambda-as-CURVATURE value free of G, but every measured Lambda-input is MEAS-G
(passes H0, M_Pl -> G at the leaf). => **(T2) COUPLING-TRANSDUCED**: pins N
only once G already known; a relabel of N in (G,m,Lambda). NOT (T3), NOT
NEW-TYPE. Type-novelty of the INPUT m_lightest does NOT transfer to the
RELATION: a G-containing equation is not rescued by a G-free input.

### DEPENDENCY GRAPH TO LEAVES (fake-G lesson operationalized)
N <- Lambda_pl,G ; Lambda_pl[MEAS-G] <- H0,M_Pl ; rho_L^{1/4}[MEAS-G] <-
rho_crit,Omega_L <- H0,M_Pl_red ; M_Pl[CONV] <- G,hbar,c ; m_sol/m_atm[MEAS]
<- oscillation data (NO G). ONLY G-free leaf = neutrino oscillation data.
Any relation outputting G/N has G on its input side through Lambda. No fitted
factor introduced; C symbolic.

## ARM B — THE DISSIPATIVE BALANCE (author-commissioned; appended by the Arm-B agent; Arm C above left intact)

### VERDICT (one line)
The de Sitter dissipative steady-state balance is EXACTLY SCALE-HOMOGENEOUS under
tau_R -> lambda tau_R. It DETERMINES the dimensionless drift ratio 1+w_eff and its
SIGN (>=0, non-crossing), and DETERMINES NOTHING on the scale direction N = tau_R(1)
= S_dS = 3pi/(Lambda G). Classified **T2 (coupling-transduced / covariant)**, NOT
NEW-TYPE. G is on BOTH sides on every branch: it cancels (holographic) or survives
as an input (intensive) — NEVER an output. The "G-free prediction" is the FINGERPRINT
of a G/G cancellation, not a derivation. The fake-G lesson operationalized.

### THE BALANCE (Sec 2 TeX)
Continuity + Clausius-sigma source, steady state on the vacuum reservoir:
    3 H (1+w_eff) rho_Lambda = Pi = T_dS * (kappa nbar^2 / delta_t) * [mode-count]
    =>  1+w_eff = Pi / (3 H rho_Lambda).
THEOREM-in-model as a rearrangement (given the RUN-28 transplant; kill-shot (i)
does not fire — the scalar Clausius on the dS crossed product is what sigma^(2)
consumes). PRODUCTION vs EXPANSION-DILUTION vs HORIZON-FLUX: the dissipative source
T_dS sigma^(2) balances the FLRW dilution term 3H(1+w)rho_L across the horizon.

### THE DEPENDENCY GRAPH TO THE LEAVES (Sec 3 TeX; verified in g31_balance.py [1])
Leaf tags: [H] Hubble rate (measured, G-free AS A RATE); [P] pure number; [G]
contains G (= M_Pl^-2 = N via the identity below).
    H       -> [H]        (leaf)
    T_dS    -> [H]        = H/2pi ; surface gravity kappa_sg = H is G-free
    beta    -> [H]        = 1/T_dS
    nbar    -> [P]        = 1/(e^{2pi w}-1) ; pure Bose occ at omega/T=2pi
    kappa   -> [P]        = 453 ; modular variance, Omega-scheme, pure
    delta_t -> [H]        = 1/(3H) ; slowest dS4 QNM Delta_+=3, G-FREE (units of H)
    sigma2  -> [P]/[H]    = (beta^2/2)<K;K>_KM ; G-free
    rho_L   -> [G]        = H^2/(8 pi G)   <-- CARRIES G (denominator)
    S_dS    -> [G]        = pi/(G H^2) = N <-- CARRIES G (holographic numerator)
**G enters at EXACTLY TWO nodes: rho_L (denom) and S_dS (holographic count).**
The QNM frequency is in units of H, NOT M_Pl => NOT a Planck-unit smuggle (home b
checked). No node bottoms out in "set by normalisation" (home a checked).

### THE STANDING IDENTITY (the fixed point; the fake-G checkpoint)
    N = tau_R(1) = S_dS = A_dS/4G = 3pi/(Lambda G) = pi/(G H^2)   [Lambda=3H^2].
Read L->R it is a DEFINITION; read R->L it is G = 3pi/(Lambda N). SAME one equation.
"deriving G" == "fixing N" == the CC problem. (ISSUES l.1860, l.3404.)

### G-POWER BOOKKEEPING — THE G/G CANCELLATION (Sec 4 TeX; sympy-verified [2])
- HOLOGRAPHIC branch: 1+w = [T_dS kappa nbar^2 S_dS] / [3H delta_t rho_L].
  S_dS = pi/(G H^2)  OVER  rho_L = H^2/(8 pi G)  =>  the two G^-1 ANNIHILATE.
  sympy: net power of G = 0, result = 4 kappa nbar^2 pi / H^3 (G-FREE by cancellation).
  ** "M_Pl^2 CANCELS" (RUN-28 headline) IS this G/G cancellation. The G-free result

- INTENSIVE branch: dissipated density ~ g_* T_dS^4 ~ H^4 (G-free) over 3H rho_L.
  sympy: net power of G = +1, result ~ G H g_* / (2 pi^3) = (H/M_Pl)^2 * pure.
  ** G SURVIVES as an INPUT: 1+w ~ 1e-122 is G-DEPENDENT (small because G is small).
     Not a derivation; G read off as input and appearing in the answer. **
On EVERY branch G is input-only (cancelled or surviving). NEVER an output.

### RESCALING PARITY, CHECKED MECHANICALLY (Sec 5 TeX; verified [3])
Under tau_R -> lambda tau_R (a STATE/normalisation rescaling):
- Every leaf {H,T_dS,beta,nbar,kappa,delta_t,rho_L} has multiplicative trace-weight
  q = 0 (geometry/temperature/pure-number objects are trace-BLIND).
- S_dS = N is the ENTROPY FACE: q=0 multiplicatively but shifts ADDITIVELY, N->N+log lam.
- sigma^(2) = (beta^2/2)<K;K>_KM has q = 0 AND no additive shift: it is a KMS-STATE
  functional (omega(1)=1 preserved for any lambda; beta,H_matter are not trace objects).
  LEMMA (KM-variance invariance): rho -> rho/lambda compensates tau_R -> lambda tau_R
  so every omega-expectation is lambda-blind. => sigma^(2) STRICTLY INVARIANT.
- THE OBSERVABLE: writing 1+w as a function of N (via G = pi/(N H^2)), sympy shows
  1+w does NOT depend on N (holographic): the additive shift N->N+log lam is INVISIBLE.
  => q[1+w_eff] = 0. The balance is COVARIANT.
- THE "DISSIPATION IS ODD" WORRY, RESOLVED MECHANICALLY: sigma^(2) is odd as a
  CUMULANT ORDER (=2, a variance, vanishing at 1st order = Clausius saturation) but
  EVEN (q=0) under the TRACE SCALE. These are ORTHOGONAL gradings (state-cumulant
  expansion vs normalisation orbit). The odd cumulant order does NOT couple to
  lambda => does NOT break T2-covariance. THE DOOR THE BRIEF PROBED IS CLOSED
  MECHANICALLY, not by fiat.

### CIRCULARITY AUDIT (Sec 6 TeX; enumerated [4]) — no CIRC, no FAKE
  C1 rho_L=M_Pl^2 H^2 (denom)        OK      G input; no claim to output G
  C2 (H/M_Pl)^2 intensive            OK      G input read into answer; G-dependent

No step smuggles the target G to derive G; no leaf fitted/invented. G at exactly

### WHAT THE BALANCE DETERMINES (Sec 7 TeX)
DETERMINES: (a) the dimensionless ratio 1+w_eff(z) [quotient/shape direction,

### TRICHOTOMY + SCALE-HOMOGENEITY ADVERSARY (Sec 8 TeX; [5])
Candidate second equation = the scalar 1+w_eff = Pi/(3H rho_L). q[1+w]=0 (covariant)

### SCALE-HOMOGENEITY ADVERSARY (which theorem hypothesis does each break?)
For A, B, C the answer on ALL of {H-i, H-ii, H-iii, H-amen} is INTACT — no
violation. Notable: for Arm C, H-ii is not violated but is what BLOCKS the

### DEEP ADVERSARY — THE ONE DOOR, PROBED AND CLOSED (g31_adversary_deep.py 5/5)
The only escape is a NON-cancelling additive log-lambda. Probed three joints:
- (P1) Takesaki-Connes theta_s (tau_R o theta_s = e^{-s} tau_R) on Q_d: <Q_d> is
  theta_s-INVARIANT (state*weight product e^{beta q}e^{-beta q}=1 is s-independent);
  the trace/entropy faces carry s. WORST CASE (asymmetric band, where theta_s DOES
  shift <Q_d> by -s): the shift IS the N->N+log lam gauge (log lam=-s) and the GSL
  DIFFERENCE cancels it: (q1-s)-(q2-s)=q1-q2. Symbolic, exact.
- (P2) finiteness/self-adjointness of Q_d holds for ALL lambda>0 (Z=lam*Z0 finite;
  ratio lam-free) => finiteness = EXISTENCE of a trace, NOT its NORMALIZATION.
  "finite only in the crossed product" != "pins the scale". #38-home-(a) disarmed.
- (P3) ARM B "dissipation is odd": every connected cumulant is a ratio of traces,
  lam-invariant order-by-order (m=0); the odd order is a CUMULANT-ORDER grading
  (order 2, a variance), ORTHOGONAL to the trace-weight grading. No hidden m!=0.

gated calculation — supply a SECOND equation on N = tau_R(1) = 3pi/(Lambda G),
escaping the R10 trichotomy T1/T2/T3? Deriving G == fixing N == the CC problem.

### THE NET (the one-line answer)
NO new object escapes. N-closure RE-CONFIRMED with THREE new entries in the dead
bins. All SIX candidate equations classify (T2) coupling-transduced/covariant
(homogeneous degree 0 under tau_R -> lambda tau_R) — or NUMEROLOGY for the bare
meV^4 coincidence. NONE is (T3); NONE is NEW-TYPE. The scale-homogeneity adversary
names NO violated hypothesis on any arm. The sole structural escape — a
non-amenable N-fixing carrier, F(A_cosm) != R_+, Popa-rigid — is UNREACHED by all
three objects; each provably lives inside the amenability-preserving crossed
product (crossed product by amenable R preserves amenability; Zimmer/Connes).
N = tau_R(1) stays structurally free; G = 3pi/(Lambda N) UNCLOSES.

## ARM A — THE CORNER ANCHOR (the boost-BRST corner charge; OBJ-1)
Staged: `scratchpad/g31_corner.tex` (7pp, pdflatex EXIT=0, 0 undefined refs) +
`g31_corner.py` (42/42 checks PASS).

### THE OBJECT (inherited BINDING, runs 24-29 + run-29 ARM A2 edge_corner.tex)
The boost-BRST corner charge `Q_d := (edge contribution of Khat)`, Khat = K_om + q,
is finite and self-adjoint UNIQUELY in the Type-II crossed product
`A_cp = A_W x|_sigma R = {A_W, e^{i t(K_om+q)}}''` (run-29 ARM A2 thm:crossed;
Witten/CLPW/Ali-Suneeta/Faulkner-Speranza). In A_W (Type III_1) it does NOT
exist as an affiliated object (no trace, no density matrix — run-29 thm:III1).
The crossed product supplies the semifinite renormalized trace Tr_r = tau_R,
density matrix rho_hat ~ e^{-beta Khat}, and finite value = renormalized boost
energy / gen-entropy variation. Tr_r IS the tau_R whose scale is N: in the II_1
observer projection tau_R(1)=e^N, N=S_dS, G=3pi/(Lambda N). => the sharpest
NEW-type anchor the R10 sweep never contained.

### THE QUESTION (A-corner commission)
Write Q_d's normalization condition; test its behavior under tau_R -> lambda tau_R
(Takesaki-Connes theta_s: tau_R o theta_s = e^{-s} tau_R): covariant (T2, dead)
or anomalous (the door)? Then the thermal Kugo-Ojima Krein identity
(<N_gh>_STr = -n_bose): does the graded trace add an INDEPENDENT condition?

### VERDICT: **T2 (coupling-transduced / covariant), DEAD — cleanly. No anomaly.**
(THEOREM-in-model: 42/42 numerical checks PASS; the covariance is exact and
profile-independent; the graded-trace datum is a Z_2 sign, orthogonal to R_+.)

### (1) THE NORMALIZATION CONDITION (explicit)
    <Q_d>_rho = Tr_r(rho_hat Q_d),  rho_hat = e^{-beta(K_om+q)}/Z,
    Z = Tr_r(e^{-beta(K_om+q)}),    tau_R(1) = e^N  (II_1 count; equiv. dual
    weight Tr_r(a)=int dq e^{beta q} <q|a|q>).
Two load-bearing features: (i) it is a RATIO (rho_hat carries its own Z, so
Tr_r(rho_hat)=1); (ii) N enters ONLY additively in the entropy (CLPW form
S = beta<Khat> + S_rel + S_0; the state-indep constant S_0 "lands entirely on
A/4G and cancels in every entropy DIFFERENCE" — paper verbatim); Q_d is a
difference object, so the N-constant cancels in it.

### (2) RESCALING tau_R -> lambda tau_R — THE THREE FACES (computed, g31_corner.py S2-S3)
    charge face:   <Q_d>_rho  ->  <Q_d>_rho          INVARIANT (ratio kills lambda)
    trace face:    Z          ->  lambda Z           (carries lambda multiplicatively)
    entropy face:  S_vN       ->  S_vN + log lambda  (N -> N + log lambda; l.16788)
NUMERICS (faithful finite model of the II_infty dual trace; theta_s = w(q)->lambda w(q)):
- <Q_d> invariant to MACHINE ZERO (|d|<=1e-16) across lambda in {2,1/2,10,1e-3,1e6}
  for THREE unrelated corner profiles (linear ~q, Gaussian bump, projection).
  => profile-INDEPENDENT (property of the ratio, not the operator).
- Z/lambda constant to 1e-9; S_vN(lambda)-S_vN(1) = log lambda to 1e-15.
CLASSIFICATION: eq:Qface (numerator & denominator scale by the same lambda) IS
the definition of T2 — covariant (here homogeneity degree 0). The scale lives
on the trace/entropy faces, which are pure gauge by tau_R o theta_s = e^{-s} tau_R.

### (3) GRADED-TRACE (Kugo-Ojima Krein) CHECK (g31_corner.py S4-S5)
- Reproduced <N_gh>_STr = STr(N_gh e^{-bH})/STr(e^{-bH}) = -e^{-bw}/(1-e^{-bw})
  = -n_bose EXACTLY (w in {0.1,0.37,0.7,1.0,1.5}; |diff|<=1e-18). This is the
  run-23 identity (indefinite metric cancels the quartet, NOT a Bose=Fermi match).
- lambda-INVARIANT: value unchanged to 1e-12 across lambda in {2,1/2,1e6,1e-6}
  AND for lambda<0 (admissible in the Krein/indefinite metric).
- DIMENSION COUNT (the decisive point): trace-scaling freedom = R_+ (1-param,
  theta_s, s in R). Krein grading datum = Z_2 (sign (-1)^F, homogeneity 0). A
  0-dim Z_2 constraint CANNOT pin a point in the 1-dim R_+ torsor.
- The two graded "normalizers" both fail to be scale-fixing: STr(e^{-bH}) over a
  quartet = Z_phys=1 (cancellation), ratio-blind; STr(1) over a quartet = 0
  (carries NO count — cannot replace tau_R(1)=e^N on the physical algebra).
=> The graded trace's consistency adds a Z_2 SIGN datum ORTHOGONAL to the scale.
   NO second equation on N. T2 survives the grading.

### THE SCALE-HOMOGENEITY ADVERSARY — CLASSIFIED, NOT ESCAPED (g31_corner.py S6)
An escape must name which hypothesis Q_d violates. It violates NONE:
- HYP-A (trace unique up to scale): NO — Q_d is DEFINED via that trace; it
  presupposes HYP-A. (Asserting a violation = #38-home-(a): the CHOSEN
  normalization tau_R(1)=1 redressed as a "derived" condition.)
- HYP-B (amenable => F(A_cosm)=R_+ => N gauge): NO — A_cp = A_W x| R is the
  crossed product of the amenable/hyperfinite wedge algebra by the amenable
  group R; amenability is PRESERVED under crossed product by an amenable group
  (Zimmer). F(A_cp)=R_+ persists. (Consistent with paper l.16823: A_cosm is
  "the hyperfinite — equivalently injective, equivalently amenable — II_1
  factor.") Q_d lives INSIDE the amenable carrier, not on a non-amenable one.
- HYP-C (state-level / difference): NO — Q_d is the renormalized boost
  ENERGY / gen-entropy VARIATION (thm:crossed(a): "modulo a state-independent
  constant"); the N-constant cancels in it. Exactly O2 (difference-cancellation).
NEW-in-origin != NEW-in-type: the classifying question is the RESCALING
behavior, and Q_d's is degree-0 covariance = T2. The graded structure gives a
Z_2 refinement, not a new real dimension. The only structural door
(non-amenable carrier, F != R_+ = the theorem's exact contrapositive) is NOT
reached by the amenability-preserving crossed product.

### CIRCULARITY AUDIT (every G/N step to the leaves)
Sole N-appearance = tau_R(1)=e^N, an INPUT normalization (not an output). No
step derives N; no G imported via Planck units / M_Pl / a QNM frequency in
Planck units. The mode frequency w in the KO identity is a BARE dimensionless
model frequency (units beta=2pi), enters only through the lambda-blind ratio,
drops out of every scale statement. NO G on either side of any equation.

### #38 LANDMINES (grades per piece)
- (a) definition posing as a condition: CONFRONTED and NAMED in-file (the #38
  box in S.norm). The normalization is a CHOICE; Arm A does not "derive" it.
- (b) G hidden in an input: AVOIDED. One N-leaf (the input normalization), no
  G-leaf; w bare not Planckian.
- (c) binary softened to a tunable: N/A (Arm C's gate). Arm A's outcome is a
  classification and lands firmly T2, not softened.
NO overclaim caught this arm (the natural overclaim — "the corner charge is
finite ONLY in the crossed product, therefore its finiteness pins the trace
scale" — is exactly the #38-(a)/(c) trap and is REJECTED: finiteness is a
RATIO property, degree-0 under rescaling; the crossed product supplies a
trace, not an absolute normalization of it).

### DISCIPLINE
Ceiling formal-pAQFT / finite-dim faithful models of the trace-scaling
automorphism + Krein graded trace (same ceiling as runs 21-29; BOTH structures
represented EXACTLY, not approximated: theta_s = w(q)->lambda w(q); KO identity
= 2x2 Grassmann super-trace). No {C_n}; no critical-DOS; no fitted factor.
g_N=GM/R^2 UNTOUCHED (n=0 leg, independent). Paper: LEDGER-ONLY.
ZERO writes to unification.tex. ZERO writes to ISSUES.md (staged: g31_corner.tex,
g31_corner.py, this ledger file).

### ONE-LINE BOTTOM LINE
The boost-BRST corner charge — the framework's sharpest new-type anchor,
finite ONLY in the Type-II crossed product — transforms COVARIANTLY (degree 0)
under tau_R -> lambda tau_R: its physical value is a trace RATIO that kills
lambda, the scale lives on the gauge trace/entropy faces (N -> N + log lambda),
and the thermal Kugo-Ojima Krein identity adds only a scale-invariant Z_2 sign.
CLASSIFIED T2, DEAD, cleanly — OBJ-1 supplies no second equation on N; the CC
problem's N-freedom survives the corner charge intact.

# Ledger — RUN 32 (R-refuter): Does M -> E_star saturation produce a horizon?

Status: **CLEAN KILL (full value).** Staged only. ZERO writes to
`unification.tex` / `ISSUES.md`.

Artifacts (all under scratchpad):
- `sat_refute.tex` -> `sat_refute.pdf` (4 pp, compiles clean, pdflatex exit 0)
- `sat_refute.py` (scales, units exhibited, no fitted factors; run verified)
- `ledger_run32.md` (this file)

---

## Claim under test (author's physical reading, NOT framework-proved)
"The framework's upper mass-energy bound is the black-hole threshold:
matter approaching M = E_star forms a horizon / becomes an
entropy-preserving black hole."

## Grep result (honesty gate)
No such claim exists in `unification.tex`. `grep saturat` -> the only
"black holes = saturation" sentence is l.6144, which is **modular /
thermal-time** flow saturation (Connes-Rovelli, Bisognano-Wichmann
horizon modular flow, Wall GSL), NOT the matter band edge E_star. The
paper does NOT say saturating the band produces a horizon. Object of the
run confirmed to be an unproved interpretation.

## Verified ground truth (located + read)
- G1  Pos planck-cutoff (l.7343): E_star is a spectral projector
      P_{<=E_star}=E_{Ĥ_m}([0,E_star]) on the MATTER algebra. Kinematic
      ceiling; propagation defined only inside it (l.7362). No evolution
      drives states toward the edge.
- G2  prop:gen-collapse (l.1827): trigger is the record filtration
      \overline{F}^γ_{τf-} via Lévy upward theorem. E_star does NOT
      appear. Collapse is record/decoherence-driven, E_star-independent.
- G3  cor:horizon (l.1888): horizon is an INPUT (observer class outside
      a pre-existing horizon); residual is Shannon H(K|G) <= log|K|,
      = 0 iff K is G-measurable. No area, no 1/G, no A/4G.
- G4  prop:no-fundamental-G (l.6740): g := (E_star/E_Pl)^2 =
      G E_star^2/(ħc^5), content-fixed g≈0.42, E_star≈0.65 E_Pl (l.6777);
      Bekenstein coeff 1/4Għ tied to E_star "alone" (H*1, l.6759).

## The three kills (each independent; all land)

### KILL 1 — Deflationary (category error)
E_star is a projector edge; a horizon is a causal/modular metric object.
Hitting the top of [0,E_star] is kinematic (no room above the band), and
the only collapse machinery is record-driven + E_star-independent (G2).
Residual entropy that would be inherited is Shannon H(K|G) (classical,
bounded by log|K|, defined with NO horizon present) — NOT Bekenstein
A/4Għ. Numbers exhibited: at r_s(E_star), Bekenstein S/k_B ~ 5 (a metric
horizon property); H(K|G) fixed by {E_k}, no G in it. Different objects.
Verdict: REFUTED. Kills overclaim-home (a)+(c).

### KILL 2 — Scale-mismatch (Planck-scale tautology)
r_s(E_star) = 2G E_star/c^4 = 2(E_star/E_Pl) ℓ_Pl ≈ 1.30 ℓ_Pl
≈ 2.1e-35 m. So "M->E_star forms a horizon" = "a ~Planck-mass lump in
~ℓ_Pl is a Planck-size black hole" — true in ANY G,ħ,c theory,
framework-independent, content-free. Ordinary matter is nowhere near the
edge per mode: proton/E_star ≈ 1.2e-19, LHC/E_star ≈ 1.7e-15. (Macro
1 kg is not a single state at 7e7 E_star; ~1e25 nucleons each ~1e-19
E_star; per-state total-energy projector, l.7413 — flagged in .py to
block the misread.) Verdict: REFUTED. Kills overclaim-home (a).

### KILL 3 — Circularity (definitionally pre-loaded)
r_s(E_star)/ℓ_Pl = 2 E_star sqrt(G/(ħc^5)) = 2 E_star/E_Pl = 2 sqrt(g),
and g := G E_star^2/(ħc^5) IS the content-fixed anchor (G4). Numeric =
symbolic = 1.2961 (machine-precision match). "E_star sits at the horizon
scale" was PUT IN when G was slaved to E_star via g; it is a definitional
identity, not a dynamical prediction. Verdict: REFUTED. Kills the
"E_star is the horizon scale = prediction" reading.

## What the claim WOULD need (Claim in sat_refute.tex §2)
A dynamical map under the framework's own evolution driving E→E_star on
a region to (i) trapped-surface/horizon formation AND (ii) A/4Għ =
H(K|G), with Type-II trace making it entropy-preserving. Paper has NO
such map. Gap = the entire dynamical content.

## Grades vs the 3 presumed overclaim-homes (#39)
- (a) horizon-from-coincidence: REFUTED (kinematic Kill 2 + definitional
      Kill 3; no dynamical trigger, G1/G2).
- (b) record-collapse == energy-threshold collapse: REFUTED (G2; l.6144
      is modular Type-III flow, different sector).
- (c) "entropy-preserving black hole" established: REFUTED (trace
      property real; BH part = unproved horizon; residual is Shannon G3).

## Honesty notes (no new overclaim introduced)
- Type-II trace / entropy-preservation is a REAL feature of the
  crossed-product sector (not attacked). Only the "black hole" splice is.
- The l.6144 modular-saturation sentence is a legitimate paper claim;
  the kill is against CONFLATING it with the matter band edge, not
  against it.
- No fitted factors. g≈0.42 is quoted from the paper, not fit.
  All other numbers are constants or elementary Schwarzschild.
- Recommended ISSUES.md entry (NOT written here): record "M->E_star
  produces a horizon" as NON-THEOREM / category error; safe to state
  only the definitional r_s(E_star)~ℓ_Pl (which is 2 sqrt(g), content-
  free), never a dynamical horizon-formation claim, absent Claim §2.

---

## ADDENDUM — ARM A2 (horizon matching), deliverable `sat_horizon.{tex,py}`

The block above is the A1/deflationary artifact (`sat_refute.*`). This
addendum records the A2-focused deliverable named by the task:
`sat_horizon.tex` (compiles clean, 4 pp, pdflatex exit 0) +
`sat_horizon.py` (runs; all scales SI, CODATA, no fitted factors).
Independent recompute AGREES with `sat_refute.py` to machine precision:
r_s(E_star)/ℓ_Pl = 1.2961 = 2√g, Bekenstein S ≈ 5.278 nats. Both artifact
sets kept; neither clobbered.

A2 adds the explicit MATCHING analysis to `cor:horizon` and the graded
"precise statement the claim would need":

- **New scale (not in A1 block): the r_s = λ_C crossover.** A self-confined
  lump traps when r_s(E) ≥ λ_C(E) = ħc/E; equality at E = E_Pl/√2 = 0.707
  E_Pl. E_star = 0.648 E_Pl sits on its shoulder. So the "horizon-formation"
  energy for a Compton-confined lump IS ~E_Pl — reinforcing Kill 2 (Planck-
  scale tautology) and Kill 3: r_s(E_star)/λ_C(E_star) = 2g exactly.

- **Two matching mismatches vs cor:horizon (staged (M1),(M2)):**
  - (M1) magnitude/dimension: S_BH[r_s(E_star)] ≈ 5.3 nats (geometric, area-
    fixed, O(1)) vs H(K|G) (unbounded decoherence conditional entropy, NO
    area term). cor:horizon never mentions area/G/E_star. NO equality regime.
  - (M2) provenance: cor:horizon ASSUMES the horizon (M_acc GIVEN) and
    computes hidden info behind it; it does not FORM one and is not triggered
    by M→E_star. The claim needs a horizon PRODUCED by saturation.

- **Type-II "entropy-preserving" half explicitly split:** "entropy-
  preserving" = real crossed-product Type-II trace (conj:horizon-alg l.5574,
  prop:E1-trace-finiteness; Killing horizons, JSS/KLS/Faulkner–Speranza) =
  HALF-EARNED. "Black hole" (saturation-formed horizon) = UNPROVED. The
  compound phrase overstates (presumed-home #39(c)).

- **Precise statement the claim WOULD need (staged Claim "required"):** a
  dynamical map forming a trapped surface A ≃ 4π r_s(E_star)² as OUTPUT of
  M→E_star; M_acc DERIVED so G's hidden sector = formed-horizon exterior;
  and H(K|G) = A/4Għ + Type-II corrections, preserved along the trace.
  Three absent ingredients: (a) trigger keyed to E_star−M; (b) derivation of
  M_acc; (c) dimensional bridge H(K|G) ↔ A/4Għ.

- **A2 grades:** saturation dynamics = FAR (record-driven, E_star-indep);
  horizon matching = FAR + partly definitional; "entropy-preserving BH"
  phrase = HALF-EARNED / HALF-EMPTY.

**Combined A1+A2 verdict: NEGATIVE-RESULT / CLEAN KILL.** All three refuters
land. Honest framework statement: E_star kinematically caps sub-Planckian EFT
matter and, via g=O(1), sets the Planckian scale at which a lump would be its
own horizon — a coincidence of the anchor, NOT a dynamical horizon-formation
result, and NOT a match to the record-theoretic residual entropy of
cor:horizon.

5.278 nats). Adds two sharpenings not in the blocks above:

- **A1-NEW-1 (the operator-algebraic ROOT of the orthogonality).** The reason
  gen-collapse is E_star-independent is not incidental: the modular
  Hamiltonian `−log Δ_ω` (STATE property; drives thermal-time + collapse +
  the l.6144 "black holes = entropy-flow saturation") and the matter
  Hamiltonian `Ĥ_m` (algebra generator; cut off by E_star) are DISTINCT
  operators — stated verbatim at **l.16604–16608** (prop:E1-trace-finiteness):
  "trace-finiteness of spectral bands of the former implies no band-limit on
  the latter"; echoed l.16628–16631. So the collapse/BH-saturation content
  lives on the MODULAR side, E_star on the MATTER-SPECTRUM side; they cannot
  be identified. This is the structural reason (C) can't be read off the
  collapse propositions (upgrades KILL 1 / home (b) from "different sector"

- **A1-NEW-2 (the paper's own M→Planck treatment refutes horizon-formation).**

  the Planck scale (Oppenheimer–Snyder), frames the crossing as matter
  EXITING the E_star scope in finite proper time — used to motivate the
  minimal clock increment δ ≳ ħ/E_star, explicitly "as motivation, not
  derivation" (l.15419). This is the OPPOSITE of a horizon-FORMING dynamical
  law at the band edge: the paper reads the approach to Planck density as a
  breakdown of the time parameter / scope exit, never as horizon nucleation
  keyed to M=E_star. Directly corroborates "E_star = kinematic ceiling."

- **A1 grade (per piece):** kinematic-vs-dynamical = KINEMATIC ceiling
  (REFUTED for dynamical); trigger = record/decoherence-driven, E_star-indep
  (high confidence, from proofs + l.16604); r_s(E_star) = 2√g ℓ_Pl content-
  free for non-Planck-compressed matter (E_hor(R)/E_star = R/(2√g ℓ_Pl),
  R=1m→4.8e34). No fitted factors; g,str[k1] used as the paper states them.

Three artifact sets now on disk (sat_refute.*, sat_horizon.*, sat_dynamics.*),
all agreeing; none clobbered.

# RUN 33 — ARM A2 (THE IDENTIFICATION) — STAGED LEDGER

**STAGED ONLY. ZERO writes to unification.tex AND ISSUES.md.**
Artifacts (this arm): `leak_sigma.tex` (compiles, 3pp, EXIT 0),
`leak_sigma.py` (all three tests, EXIT 0). unification.tex + ISSUES.md
untouched by this arm.

## ONE-LINE VERDICT
The $O(\mu)$ cone-leakage and $\sigma^{(2)}=\tfrac{\beta^2}{2}\langle K;K\rangle_{KM}$
are **INDEPENDENT** — different sector, dimension, small-parameter, and
character. They do NOT coincide and are NOT proportional. The author's
"lossy energy conservation = the $\sigma$-production" reading is a
**category error on all three axes**. GRADE: **NEGATIVE-RESULT** (full value).

## THE QUESTION (base task A2)
Is the $O(\mu)$ cone-leakage the SAME object as the $\sigma^{(2)}$ entropy
production? COINCIDE (one object, two descriptions), PROPORTIONAL (computable
factor), or INDEPENDENT (different sectors, author's conflation)?

## THE TWO OBJECTS (verified from source)
| | cone-leakage (L) | $\sigma^{(2)}$ production (S) |
|---|---|---|
| origin | $\widehat H_{eff}=\widehat H_s-\tfrac\mu2\widehat H_s^2$, nonlocal term | interacting $:\!\phi^4\!:$ on horizon |
| tex/ISSUES | tex l.16215, l.4450, l.10597 | ISSUES l.5051/5118/5260; tex BW l.4053 |
| locus | $E_\star$ band-limit / record cone | Rindler/dS **horizon** (BW modular, type-III$_1$) |
| order | $O(\mu)=O(\alpha\,\delta t^2/\hbar^2)$ | $O(\lambda_{int}^2)$ in $\lambda_{int}\phi^4$ |
| dimension | $t^{-1}$ (rate) / operator defect | **entropy** (nat, dimensionless) |
| small param | $\mu$ (**inverse energy**) | $\lambda_{int}$ (coupling) |
| character | off-diagonal / **dissipative** ((M3b) defect) | $\nu=0$ diagonal / **reactive** (run-30 razor) |
| leaking qty | (M3b) micro-causality localization | — ($S_{rel}$ 2nd variation; a state functional, leaks nothing) |
| vanishes when | reduction (exact statistics, tex l.16219) | never ($\ge0$, onsets $n{=}2$) |

## THE THREE COMPUTED SEPARATIONS (each independently decisive)

### T-DIM — DIMENSIONAL MISMATCH (kills refuter-ii direction: identification IS a category error)
- $[\mu]=E^{-1}$ (tex l.10598 verbatim "units of inverse energy").
- cone-leak $[\mu E_\star^2/\hbar]=E^{-1}E^2(Et)^{-1}=t^{-1}$ = a RATE.
- $K=\beta H$ dimensionless $\Rightarrow \sigma^{(2)}$ dimensionless (entropy/nat).
- A rate and an entropy are incommensurable; converting needs BOTH a clock
  AND a $k_BT$ that neither object carries. **Category error confirmed.**

### T-MU — THE $\mu\leftrightarrow\lambda$ ALGEBRA (the crux; kills home-(b))
- **TWO distinct $\lambda$'s**: $\lambda_{TDSE}:=\alpha\,\delta t/\hbar$
  (DIMENSIONLESS subcriticality, tex l.4532/10611/15473/16267) vs
  $\lambda_{int}$ (the $\phi^4$ coupling controlling $\sigma^{(2)}$).
- **DERIVED (exact, not fitted):** $\lambda_{TDSE}^2=\alpha\,\mu$, i.e.
  $\mu=\lambda_{TDSE}^2/\alpha$ [verified numerically 4 points, T-MU].
- So "$\mu\sim\lambda^2$" is TRUE for $\lambda_{TDSE}$ (clock deformation)
  and UNDEFINED for $\lambda_{int}$ (matter). $\mu$ is set by $(\alpha,\delta t)$
  = clock micro-structure ($\widehat H_c$); $\lambda_{int}$ is set by the
  interacting $\widehat H_s$. Independent theory inputs — no bridge derivable
  or claimed; the shared glyph $\lambda$ is the whole tie.
- Planck point: $\mu=1/E_P$, leak $\sim\langle H_s\rangle/E_P\sim10^{-28}$ at 1 eV
  (Planck-suppressed, clock sector); $\sigma^{(2)}\sim44$ ($O(1)$ in $\lambda_{int}$,
  horizon sector). **Home (b) — the dimensional-analysis conflation — avoided.**

### T-CHAR — REACTIVE vs DISSIPATIVE (kills refuter-iii direction: razor forbids the identification)

- Off-diagonal ($dN\ne0$) monomials $a,a^\dagger,a^2,a^{\dagger2}$: $\langle K;X\rangle_{KM}=0$
  identically (K diagonal, X off-diagonal, no KM overlap) [reproduced 0.0 machine prec, T-CHAR].
- A leak IS an off-diagonal dissipative channel (Im $\Sigma$, linewidth).
  $\sigma^{(2)}$ has "**no dissipative channel**" (run-30, ISSUES l.6195/6486).
  **So $\sigma^{(2)}$ cannot BE the cone-leak — refuter (iii) fires.**

## THE THREE REFUTERS — ALL THREE FIRE (each is a real separation)
- (ii) dimensional-mismatch kill: CONFIRMED (T-DIM) — rate vs entropy, cutoff vs horizon.
- (iii) reactive-character kill: CONFIRMED (T-CHAR) — run-30 razor forbids a dissipative leak.

## HEADLINE
Author's premise ("lossy energy conservation because of its boundary, and that
loss IS sigma^(2)") is **REFUTED on both halves**. GRADE: **NEGATIVE-RESULT**
(full value). All three refuters FIRE.

## (i) EXACT-CONSERVATION KILL — DECISIVE
thm:conservation (l.1060; proof l.1090-1106) uses ONLY exact unitary covariance
U(tau,tau') [true H(tau)], cyclicity (-> x-independent rho_2(tau_i|k)), and the
operator law nabla^mu O=0. H_eff/mu/eps/E* appear NOWHERE. EXACT & unconditional
on its class. Every leak lives OUTSIDE that class:
- cutoff eps^{1/2}: removable STATE approx error, eps~e^{-beta E*}->0 (l.7638);
  the ENERGY defect is O(eps) — quadratic in leakage, 1st-order shifts vanish
  (off-diag P-Q blocks, l.7666-76).
- cone O(mu E*^2/hbar): the FUNDAMENTAL e^{-is C/hbar} acts on observables
  EXACTLY as conj by e^{-is H_s/hbar} (M2/M3b inherited verbatim, l.16195-200);
  H_eff's M3b defect is a dt-truncation ARTIFACT (l.16221), not energy loss.
- "non-Hermitian before reduction": delay-advance TDSE (prop:tdse) not per-slice
  norm-preserving, but unitary on HISTORY space (Kato-Rellich). Reduction
  H_eff=H_s-(mu/2)H_s^2 is SELF-ADJOINT for every real mu (l.4453). The order-1
  non-Herm factor is the ASYMMETRIC variant, RULED OUT by spectroscopy
  (l.4530-34). No Lindblad (l.9840-57).
DECISIVE (prop:tdse-effective ii, l.15500): the EXACT conditional evolution is
iħ ∂_t ψ = h(H_s) ψ with h(H_s) SELF-ADJOINT (part i). Conservation is not even
"conditionally leaky at O(mu)"; the exact conditional generator is self-adjoint
to all orders on the slow branch. H_eff is its leading Taylor truncation.
=> "lossy energy conservation" = FALSE. Nothing physical leaks.

## (ii) DIMENSIONAL-MISMATCH KILL
cone-leak: dimensionless op-norm defect (rate mu E*^2/hbar ~ t^{-1}); O(mu),
[mu]=1/energy; E* cutoff; gravity/clock. sigma^(2): entropy (k_B); O(lambda_int^2);
horizon KMS; matter. THE lambda-CRUX (adopt+reverify A2): mu DOES satisfy
**lambda_TDSE^2 = alpha*mu** (exact, both dimensionless; re-derived in
leak_refute.py) — but lambda_TDSE:=alpha dt/hbar is the CLOCK parameter, NOT the
matter lambda_int of sigma^(2). Shared glyph lambda is the whole illusion;
lambda_TDSE != lambda_int, no bridge to sigma^(2). Independent sectors, different
vanishing limits (E*->inf vs lambda_int->0). => identification FAILS dimensionally.

## (iii) REACTIVE-CHARACTER KILL (run-30 razor, reproduced from scratch)
leak_refute.py: S_prod/W_diag = 1.000000; production = EXACTLY the nu=0 REACTIVE
(dN=0) diagonal Kubo-Mori weight; off-diagonal (dN=+/-2, nu!=0) channel = 0. A
leak IS a dissipative off-diagonal flux (linewidth); run-30: sigma^(2)=reactive
half, Gamma_BL=dissipative half, overlap 0, sigma^(2) has NO dissipative channel.
=> sigma^(2) CANNOT be a (dissipative) leak, regardless of dimensions. FAILS on razor.

## THE IDENTIFICATION (T3): INDEPENDENT
Not coincident (dimensionless vs entropy); not proportional (mu ∝ lambda_TDSE^2
CLOCK, not lambda_int MATTER; independent vanishing limits); not two views of one
effect (E* cutoff vs horizon; truncation-artifact/reactive vs reactive, one
dissipative one not). Excluded on 3 independent grounds. sigma^(2) is provably
NOT dissipative at all. Honest deflationary answer = full value; genuine
identification would have been an internal unification, but the structure forbids it.

|---|---|---|---|

| **L3** two-boundary τ_i/τ_f records (l.3229) | **information about outcome K** | exact: M_τ ⊆ F̄_τ^γ | statistical no-signalling (**theorem**, not a defect). Not energy. |

the proof. => EXACT and unconditional ON its class (nabla-conserved observables in
conditioned expectations). STRENGTHENED by prop:tdse-effective(ii) (l.15500-15502): the
EXACT conditional generator on the slow branch is h(H_s), SELF-ADJOINT (l.15496) — so
conservation is not even "conditionally leaky at O(mu)"; H_eff=H_s-(mu/2)H_s^2 (l.4450)
is merely its self-adjoint leading truncation. The three leakage loci are DISTINCT
non-energy quantities OUTSIDE the class:
  L1 cutoff P_{<=E*} (l.7638): amplitude eps^{1/2} (Theta-sharp, l.7656-64); conserved
     eigenvalue/energy budget O(eps) QUADRATIC — first-order shifts vanish identically

--- #40 HOME (b): the mu-vs-lambda^2 relation ---
CONFIRMED, with the conflation exposed. There are TWO lambdas.
  DERIVED EXACT: lambda_TDSE^2 = alpha*mu  (adj_run33.py B, symbolic residual = 0), where
    lambda_TDSE := alpha*dt/hbar is DIMENSIONLESS and is the CLOCK Banach-contraction

  BUT lambda_int = the phi^4 coupling g in K=(g/4!)int x^1:phi^4: (the DFP boost cocycle,

    matter H_s on the horizon. Independent theory inputs; the shared glyph lambda is the

    lambda. DIMENSIONS: [mu]=E^-1 (l.4426); cone-leak RATE [mu E*^2/hbar]=T^-1 (verified);
    sigma^(2)=(beta^2/2)<K;K>_KM is a dimensionless ENTROPY (nat). RATE vs ENTROPY
    incommensurable => identifying them is a category error. The author's "mu conflated with
    lambda^2 by dimensional analysis without deriving their relation" (home b) is EXACTLY the
    trap: the derivable relation ties mu to the WRONG lambda.

--- #40 HOME (c): sigma-leak vs run-30's reactive-diagonal razor ---
CONFIRMED — a reactive object CANNOT be a dissipative leak. Reproduced the razor from
scratch (adj_run33.py A, single-mode Fock/KMS, imports numpy only):

  regardless of dimensions. The identification fails on the razor, home (c) confirmed.

(the prior adjudicator note above already flagged this, non-load-bearing).

GRADE per piece: (a) CONFIRMED full grade. (b) CONFIRMED full grade. (c) CONFIRMED full
grade. Overall: leak and sigma^(2) INDEPENDENT; "lossy energy conservation" FALSE.

  mu / eps / E_star / H_eff appear NOWHERE in the proof. Class = nabla-conserved Heisenberg
  observables in conditioned expectations Tr(rho_2(tau|k) O_S). STRENGTHENED by

  (l.15495-97) -> conservation is not even conditionally-leaky at O(mu). REPRODUCED (check 0):
  conserved obs constant to 1.9e-16 under exact h(H_s); non-conserved obs varies 1.35 (outside
  the class). VERDICT: conservation exact WHERE claimed; leakage lives OUTSIDE the class.

(2) LEAKAGE ANATOMY (per-locus verdict):
  L1 cutoff P_{<=E*} (l.7580-7703): leaks probability AMPLITUDE at eps^{1/2} (Theta-SHARP,

    by Hyp:eps-tails (un-closed T5 clause, l.7644-52) for REMOVABILITY only, not the verdict.
  L2a modified-TDSE generator (l.4450): H_eff=H_s-(mu/2)H_s^2 SELF-ADJOINT for every real mu
    (l.4453) -> leaks NOTHING; energy+norm exactly conserved. The order-1 non-Hermitian per-

    the asymmetric non-Hermitian variant is spectroscopically EXCLUDED (l.4530-34). REPRODUCED
    (check 2): Hermitian defect 0.0 exactly; norm+energy drift ~1e-15.

    explicit "artifact of truncating the delta_t-expansion" (l.16221). Exact e^{-isC/hbar}

  L3 two-boundary records (l.3229): leaks INFORMATION about outcome K (M_tau subset Fbar_tau),

  conservation" = FALSE. Refuter (i) FIRES.

(3) LEAKAGE = sigma^(2)?  INDEPENDENT (neither coincide nor proportional).
  - DIMENSION/ORDER (refuter ii): cone-leak coefficient mu E*^2/hbar has units 1/time (a RATE)

    dimensionless is an ENTROPY (nat). RATE vs ENTROPY incommensurable. O(mu) vs O(lambda_int^2).

  - mu vs lambda^2 (home b, THE CONFLATION): there are TWO lambdas. lambda^2 = alpha*mu is

(4) AUTHOR'S "lossy because of its boundary": FALSE (removable approximation).

# Ledger — RUN 34, ARM A3-cost: THE CLAUSE AUDIT

**Commission.** Read what the n=1 clause ACTUALLY consumes
(thm:E2-Lipschitz, the T1'-consumption / slice-ladder sites,
cor:newtonian-limit-V1). Is the crossed-product restatement FREE or COSTLY?

**VERDICT (one line).** The clause audit **DISSOLVES the bridge question**:
the three consumer sites named in the commission (E2-Lipschitz, the T1'
slice feeders, cor:newtonian-limit-V1) **do not consume the fixed-background
one-sided K_omega at all** — the paper's OWN text certifies the deliverable
is derived WITHOUT the n=1 first-law identity. The one site that DOES carry
the fixed-background K_omega (thm:clausius-SM eq:clausius-SM-flux, and the
n=1 boost-Noether upgrade R-i it would need to go pointwise) is a
DIFFERENT clause from the deliverable, and there the restatement is NOT
free: the crossed-product move is O(G) gravitational dressing and cannot
discharge the O(hbar) fixed-background locality the identity literally
asserts. So: **restatement FREE for the deliverable (it never needed the
object), COSTLY for the SM first-law identity (order-mismatch, refuter (i)
lands).** The A3 gate does not close by reformulation; but the deliverable
cor:newtonian-limit-V1 was never gated on it. This CONFIRMS run-29's exact
finding and closes the question NEGATIVELY-but-cleanly: the gap cannot be
bridged, AND the deliverable does not need it bridged.

Grade: **NEGATIVE-RESULT (audit-certified from the paper's own text).**

## THE MAP: three consumer classes, three DISTINCT objects

Located by grep + read of unification.tex (line numbers exact):

### CLASS 1 — thm:E2-Lipschitz (l.17461-17537). Object: NEITHER charge.
The theorem bounds the matter stress-tensor expectation Lipschitz-in-metric:
    |<That^m_{munu}[f]>_{omega_g} - <...>_{omega_g'}| <= L(g0,K) ||f||_{H^s} ||g-g'||_{H^s}.
Proof (four parts) consumes ONLY: (1) point-splitting operator coefficient
bounds D_*, (2) Hadamard jet bounds C_W (Hyp hadamard-uniform (ii)), (3) the
Theta renormalisation curvature polynomial (four-jets, s>n/2+4), (4) volume
form. VERBATIM l.17486-17487:
    "In particular L(g0,K)<infty: **no QEI constant and no modular norm
     enters the Lipschitz modulus.**"
=> E2-Lipschitz is ORTHOGONAL to the boost charge entirely. It consumes
   neither K_omega (fixed-background, non-affiliated) NOR the Type-II corner
   charge. It is a Sobolev-jet estimate on <T>, not a modular object.
   The commission's premise "the n=1 clause = E2-Lipschitz consumes K_omega"
   is FALSE at the source. E2-Lipschitz has nothing to restate.

### CLASS 2 — the T1' slice feeders (l.17179-17269). Object: NEITHER charge.
T1' = the half-derivative booking partial_y:(c,p)->(c+1/2,p-1) that promotes
the Sobolev slice register (Option A: s>11 over analytic collar U_omega;
Option B: s>11.5 over full H^s ball). Consumes: analytic fold-count, linear
sublevel device on the fold stratum, near-flat-sliver weigh-in
(Thm an17:E2a-omega-main Part A). These are microlocal REGULARITY inputs
feeding E2-Lipschitz's constants. NO modular object, NO boost charge.
=> Also orthogonal. Nothing to restate.

### CLASS 3 — the n=1 SM first-law IDENTITY. Object: THE fixed-background K_omega.
This is the ACTUAL home of the disputed object. TWO faces:
 (3a) thm:clausius-SM (l.19280-19329), eq:clausius-SM-flux (l.19316):
      delta Q := int_B <That^SM_{ab}>_omega chi^a dSigma^b = T dK_omega + hbar A^even_grav[g] + O(v^2/E_Pl^2),
      K_omega = -log Delta_omega  (l.19283, EXPLICIT fixed-background modular
      Hamiltonian). This is the INTEGRATED-over-B flux identity. It is a
      THEOREM as an integrated statement (null-surface modular theory).
 (3b) the n=1 boost-NOETHER identity (R-i) (l.19466-19469, l.19811):
      "the interacting boost charge equals 2pi int x^1 delta<T_00^SM,(1)>",
      the POINTWISE / every-null-direction upgrade. THIS is the object whose
      residual A3 = edge-locality of the descent charge Y^1 at B, the
      [x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} boundary term, the runs 24-29 wall. Exact at n=0,
      OPEN at n>=1. = companion problem B1a.

## THE AUDIT: what cor:newtonian-limit-V1 (THE DELIVERABLE) actually consumes

cor:newtonian-limit-V1 (l.19413-19478) is the deliverable named in the
commission ("independent of this [arm]"). Read it verbatim. Its LOAD-BEARING
RHS carries ONLY the exact n=0 term:
   delta G kk = (8pi G/c^4) delta<That^SM,(0)>_omega kk + R_{>=1}(x;k),
                                                         int_{S_r} R_{>=1} dS = 0.
The n=0 leg is EXACT by Bisognano-Wichmann (wedge modular Hamiltonian = boost
charge for FREE/Gaussian matter, mass-independent) + Longo/CLR free first law
(the free two-state relative modular operator GENUINELY EXISTS — the free
object is affiliated, no III_1 pathology because the n=0 leg needs the FULL
two-sided modular Hamiltonian, not a one-sided corner charge).

**THE DECISIVE VERBATIM (l.19466-19469, l.19737-19739):**
   "The n=1 interacting-matter first-law IDENTITY --- that the interacting
    boost charge equals 2pi int x^1 delta<T_00^SM,(1)> --- is NOT USED here;
    it is the interacting boost-Noether identity (R-i), exact at n=0 but
    open at n>=1 ... "
   "...classical recovery for planets is DERIVED at leading interacting order
    --- resting on the n=0 equation of state and mass conservation, NOT on
    any interacting first-law identity (the deliverable NEVER REFERENCES the
    interacting boost-Noether identity (R-i))."

=> The paper ALREADY certifies, in print, the exact A3-cost answer:
   cor:newtonian-limit-V1 does NOT consume the fixed-background one-sided
   K_omega. It consumes (i) the n=0 free modular Hamiltonian (two-sided,
   affiliated, exists), and (ii) the shell-theorem mass-conservation step
   (int R_{>=1} dS = 0), which routes the entire interacting tail into the
   measured M and leaves g_N = GM/R^2 exactly invariant.

**CONSEQUENCE FOR THE THREE ARMS (A1/A2/A3-cost):**
- The commission's framing assumed cor:newtonian-limit-V1's premise (N1)
  needs the n=1 fixed-background object, so a Type-II restatement would be
  "free iff the consumer never needed the fixed-background specifically."
- The audit finds: the consumer (the DELIVERABLE) never needed it, but not
  because a Type-II restatement suffices --- because the deliverable is
  built on the n=0 free leg + mass conservation and STRUCTURALLY BYPASSES
  the n=1 identity. This is the CHM-ball-style structural avoidance the wall
  text already flagged (l.19846: "the CHM ball avoids the gate structurally
  rather than by taper").

## THE RESTATEMENT COST — the two clauses that DO carry K_omega (3a/3b)

The restatement question only has teeth for CLASS 3 (thm:clausius-SM and its
n=1 pointwise upgrade R-i / B1a). Here the crossed-product move is NOT free.
Three reasons, each independent (they are the run's three refuters, and each
LANDS):

### COST C1 (refuter (i), the ORDER-MISMATCH kill) — LANDS.
The fixed-background K_omega = -log Delta_omega in eq:clausius-SM-flux is an
O(hbar^0)-in-G object: it is defined on A_W (the bare wedge algebra, Type
III_1) with NO reference to G or the clock. The Type-II crossed product
A_cp = A_W x|_sigma R adjoins the ADM/clock dof at O(1/N) ~ O(G) (run-29 C1:
G = 3pi/(Lambda N)). The corner charge's finiteness/affiliation is a
PROPERTY OF A_cp, i.e. an O(G) gravitational-dressing phenomenon. A charge
identity written in A_cp differs from the identity in A_W by O(G) terms.
=> The affiliation defect [x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} that A3 asks to vanish is the
   defect of the FIXED-BACKGROUND (O(G^0)) object; making the DRESSED
   (O(G)) object affiliated changes the defect only at O(G). At O(G^0) --
   the order eq:clausius-SM-flux lives at, the order the deliverable's (N1)
   premise lives at -- the fixed-background object is STILL non-affiliated,
   the defect is STILL there. A G-order affiliation cannot cancel an
   hbar-order/G^0-order divergence: they are different terms in the
   expansion. (This is the fourth cousin of the one-point/two-point trap,
   #41-home-(a), and it is REAL here.)

### COST C2 (refuter (ii), the WRONG-OBJECT kill) — LANDS.
The Type-II trace Tr_r renormalizes the boost ENERGY / generalized ENTROPY
(Wall GSL scheme, CLPW S = beta<Khat> + S_rel + S_0). A3 literally asks for
the edge-LOCALITY of the descent current Y^1 -- that [Q^(1), K^(0)_W] = 0
holds on the non-compact wedge, i.e. that the boundary term at x^1=0
vanishes. A FINITE renormalized energy does NOT make a current LOCAL at the
edge: finiteness of int is orthogonal to vanishing of the boundary term of
its IBP. (run-29 C2 verbatim: "the trace renormalizes ENERGY/ENTROPY not the
descent-current LOCALITY A3 literally asks".) The gate object is the
ghost-number-neutral TWO-POINT norm on the rho^{-4} dimensional floor
(l.19826-19828); the crossed-product trace acts on the ONE-point
charge/energy, not the two-point locality norm. Same one-point/two-point
transport failure as #32/#33/#34/#36.

### COST C3 (refuter (iii), the CIRCULARITY kill) — LANDS as a HYGIENE bound.
The crossed product adjoins the clock PRECISELY to supply a trace and make
the corner charge affiliated (run-29 thm:crossed; run-31 #38-home-a). So
"the corner charge is finite/affiliated in A_cp" is true BY CONSTRUCTION --
the affiliation is ENGINEERED (the semifinite trace is adjoined), not
DERIVED from the fixed-background dynamics. Asserting "therefore the n=1
first law closes" would redress an engineered affiliation as a derived
locality. Run-31's landmine one level up. => any A1 "lift" that claims the
descent boundary term becomes the affiliation defect of a now-affiliated
charge (=> zero defect => gate closes) is EMPTY at the fixed-background
order: it closes a defect that was never at that order to begin with (C1),
of an object made affiliated by fiat (C3), measuring energy not locality (C2).

## A2 (THE DESCENT LIMIT) — N-scaling of the corner charge finiteness, explicit

The reverse question: does Type-II finiteness DESCEND to the fixed-background
hbar-order charge as G->0 / N->infinity (A_cp -> A_W)? Compute the N-scaling.

Setup (run-29/run-31 inherited): A_cp = A_W x|_sigma R, semifinite trace
Tr_r = tau_R with tau_R(1) = e^N, N = S_dS, and G = 3pi/(Lambda N). The
corner charge Q_d is affiliated to A_cp with a finite VALUE that is a trace
RATIO <Q_d>_rho = Tr_r(rho_hat Q_d), rho_hat = e^{-beta Khat}/Z (run-31 Arm A,
eq:Qface: degree-0 covariant, N cancels in the ratio).

N-scaling of the FINITENESS (not the value):
- The affiliation of Q_d to A_cp requires the trace tau_R to EXIST. tau_R
  exists iff the crossed product is semifinite (Type II), which holds iff the
  modular automorphism sigma^omega is made inner-implementable by the adjoined
  R -- i.e. iff the clock dof is present. Presence of the clock is O(1/N)~O(G):
  the shift generator's spectrum is the ADM/clock energy, weight ~1/N.
- As N -> infinity (G -> 0): tau_R(1) = e^N -> infinity, and the crossed
  product DEGENERATES back to the bare wedge algebra A_W (the clock decouples:
  its coupling to A_W is the O(1/N) dressing). In the strict G->0 limit A_cp
  -> A_W (Type III_1), and A_W has NO trace: tau_R does not survive the limit
  as a state on A_W (a III_1 factor admits no semifinite normal trace, period
  -- Connes). The affiliation of Q_d is LOST in the limit.
- QUANTITATIVELY: the density matrix rho_hat = e^{-beta Khat}/Z has
  eigenvalue support that spreads as the clock spectrum flattens; the entropy
  S = beta<Khat> + S_rel + S_0 with S_0 -> A/4G = (Lambda N/12) -> infinity as
  N->infinity. The FINITE PART of the corner charge (the ratio) survives
  (degree-0), but the OBJECT-AS-AFFILIATED (having a density matrix at all)
  is a strictly-Type-II phenomenon: it DISAPPEARS at G=0. The one-sided
  fixed-background K_omega on A_W is left NON-AFFILIATED and its
  [x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} defect DIVERGENT (runs 24-27 quantitative shadow).

=> A2 VERDICT: the Type-II finiteness is a STRICTLY O(G) phenomenon that
   DISAPPEARS as G->0. It does NOT descend to the hbar-order fixed-background
   charge. The honest obstruction stands: the hbar-order clause (3b, R-i) is
   divergent in the limit, and no amount of G-order affiliation reaches it.
   This is the HONEST horn of the commission's A2, and it is the one that
   holds. (The finiteness is NOT a limit of the III_1 object; it is a
   property of a DIFFERENT algebra that the III_1 object is the G->0
   degeneration of.)

## A1 (THE LIFT) — can the n=1 identity be RE-DERIVED to consume Q_d? NO.
Blocked by C1+C2+C3 jointly. The lift would need the descent boundary term
[x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} to EQUAL the affiliation defect of Q_d (=> zero when Q_d
affiliated => gate closes). But:
- the closure would live at O(G) (C1: Q_d's affiliation is O(G)), whereas the
  paper's n=1 clause (3b/R-i) and the integrated identity (3a) live at O(G^0)
  fixed-background. A G-order closure is a GRAVITATIONAL CORRECTION to the
  first law, NOT a discharge of the G^0 term. It does not descend to the
  hbar-order object the identity needs (this is A2's negative result read
  forward).
- even at O(G) the closure measures ENERGY not LOCALITY (C2): affiliation-
  defect-zero = finite energy != vanishing edge boundary term of the IBP.
- and the affiliation is engineered (C3).
=> A1 CLOSURE IS EMPTY at the required order. The lift does not bridge.

## HONESTY — grades per piece; #41 presumed homes

- (a) "finite in Type-II => n=1 first law closes" transporting G-order
  affiliation to the hbar-order clause without the descent:
  CONFRONTED and REJECTED (C1 + A2). The order-mismatch is REAL and named;
  the closure, if asserted, would be the fourth cousin of the
  one-point/two-point trap. NOT committed.
- (b) affiliation asserted BY CONSTRUCTION rather than derived:
  CONFRONTED (C3). The crossed product is engineered to supply the trace;
  "Q_d finite in A_cp" is true by construction. NOT redressed as derived.
- (c) clause restatement claimed free without reading what

     them would be the overclaim, and it is AVOIDED.

NO NEW OVERCLAIM this arm. The natural overclaim -- "the deliverable doesn't
need K_omega, therefore the Type-II route closes the SM n=1 wall" -- is
EXACTLY the clause-conflation and is REJECTED: the deliverable's independence
from K_omega does NOT close the SM n=1 first-law identity; it makes the wall

## THE COMMISSION (verbatim scope)
A2 = THE DESCENT LIMIT: does the Type-II corner-charge finiteness DESCEND to
the fixed-background hbar-order K_omega in the G->0 / N->infinity limit
(A_cp -> bare wedge A_W), or is the finiteness a strictly G-order phenomenon
that DISAPPEARS as G->0 (leaving the hbar-order clause divergent = the honest
obstruction)? COMPUTE THE N-SCALING OF THE CORNER CHARGE'S FINITENESS EXPLICITLY.

## VERDICT (one line)
**STRICTLY O(G) / O(1/N). The finiteness VANISHES as G->0. It does NOT descend.**
The corner charge Q_d is finite in A_cp because the adjoined clock provides a
semifinite trace whose regulator scale is exactly N = 3pi/(Lambda G); the
G->0 limit is N->infinity, in which (a) the crossed product degenerates back to
the type-III_1 wedge A_W, (b) A_W admits NO semifinite normal trace (Connes:
a III_1 factor has none), so the object-as-affiliated evaporates, and (c) the
one-sided fixed-background K_omega on A_W is left non-affiliated with its
[x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} edge defect DIVERGENT (the runs 24-27 quantitative shadow).
The finiteness is the property of a DIFFERENT algebra of which the III_1 object
is the G->0 degeneration -- it is not a G->0 limit of the III_1 object.
Grade: **NEGATIVE-RESULT (the honest horn; THEOREM-in-model on the N-scaling).**

## THE EXPLICIT N-SCALING (computed, tii_descent.py) -- see below sections

## (S1) THE ADDRESS -- G = 3pi/(Lambda N), G->0 <=> N->infinity  [EXACT, sympy]
N_of_G = 3pi/(G Lambda) inverts G = 3pi/(Lambda N); lim_{N->oo} G = 0;
dG/dN = -3pi/(Lambda N^2) < 0. The descent limit G->0 IS N->infinity at fixed
Lambda. (unification.tex eq:G-as-address l.16712, eq:dS-ceiling l.16671.)

## (S2) THE VALUE IS DEGREE-0 (N CANCELS) -- flat, uninformative for descent
Reproduced run-31 Arm A eq:Qface from scratch (independent II_infty dual-weight
finite model): <Q_d>_rho = Tr_r(rho_hat Q_d) invariant to <=6e-17 across
lambda in {2,1/2,10,1e-3,1e6} for 3 corner profiles; Z ~ lambda (trace face);
STATE vN entropy lambda-invariant; entropy ZERO-POINT S_0=log tau_R(1)=N shifts
by log lambda. => the corner-charge VALUE's N-scaling is FLAT; it says nothing
about descent. The descent question is about the FINITENESS-AS-AFFILIATION.

## (S3) THE FINITENESS-AS-AFFILIATION IS O(1/N) ~ O(G)
- STRUCTURAL: III_1 admits no semifinite trace. Witness = modular KMS DOS
  nu(E)~e^{2pi E}: Tr(1) over [0,Emax] grows 4.6e4 -> 3.1e26 as Emax=2..10
  (diverges). => the affiliation is STRICTLY a crossed-product property; there
  is nothing on A_W to descend TO.
- clock coupling g_clock ~ 1/N = (Lambda/3pi) G: ratio g_clock/G CONSTANT in N
  => g_clock is O(G) EXACTLY; g_clock(N=1e122) ~ 1e-122 -> 0. THE CLOCK
  DECOUPLES as N->infinity; A_cp -> A_W on the physical sector.

## (S4) THE AFFILIATION DEFECT DIVERGES as N->infinity (DECISIVE)
One-sided boost-charge fluctuation over the N-regulated modular band
[0, E_max(N)], E_max=(log N)/(2pi):
  <K^2>_KMS ~ E_max^2/3 ~ (log N)^2   (normalized; fitted slope 2.0000)
  Tr(K^2) = int E^2 e^{2pi E} dE ~ e^{2pi E_max} = N   (the III_1
     non-affiliation object = the modular TRACE; fitted slope 1.0000, ~N~1/G)
Table: N=1e2->1e122, <K^2>_KMS 0.18->666, Tr(K^2) diverges to >1e100. BOTH
diverge as N->infinity => density matrix de-bounds, AFFILIATION LOST in limit.

## (S5) THE [x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} EDGE DEFECT vs N -- link to runs 24-27
E[f]^2 ~ int_{rho_c(N)}^1 rho^{2 alpha_f+2-2p} drho, run-26/27 dim floor p=2,
paper's consumer alpha_f=0 (loci 1/2/4, run-27 Door B). Crossed-product collar
rho_c(N)=N^{-a} (edge fence from the clock). Result (alpha_f=0,p=2,a=1):
  E[f]^2 = N^a - 1 ~ N ~ 1/G   (fitted slope d log E[f]^2/d log N = 1.0000)
FINITE at finite N (crossed product regulates); DIVERGES as N->infinity
(collar removed = G->0) => reopens the runs-24-27 divergence.
ROBUSTNESS (anti-circularity, refuter iii): divergence direction robust across
collar exponents a in {0.5,1,2} (only rate changes); the ONLY N-uniform-finite
alternative is an N-INDEPENDENT (fixed geometric) collar = run-27 Door B's
imposed fence that CHANGES THE THEOREM. A collar tracking the O(G) dressing must
vanish as G->0. So the divergence is NOT a collar-choice artifact.
CONTROL: tapered consumer alpha_f=2>3/2 is N-uniformly FINITE -- but the
paper's consumer is alpha_f=0.

## (S6) ORDER SEPARATION (refuter i, the ORDER-MISMATCH KILL) -- LANDS
  g_clock ~ 1/N            : O(G^1)     -> 0 as G->0
  eps ~ g'/g ~ 1/sqrt(N)   : O(G^{1/2}) -> 0 as G->0   (paper l.19506)
  K_omega = -log Delta_om  : O(G^0)     -> STAYS, non-affiliated
KILL: an O(G^{>0}) affiliation CANNOT discharge an O(G^0) divergence -- they are
different terms in the G-expansion. A G-order closure would be a GRAVITATIONAL
CORRECTION to the first law, not a cancellation of the fixed-background defect.
The two refuters "does not descend" (G->0) and "cannot lift" (G-order closure
never reaches O(G^0)) are the SAME fact from the two ends of the expansion.
[NB: the exact power (1/2 vs 1) is NOT pinned; BOTH shown; the kill holds for
either since both are POSITIVE powers of G. Honest: range O(G^{1/2..1}).]

## REFUTERS (ii)/(iii) -- for completeness
(ii) WRONG-OBJECT: paper l.12292 "only the trace/entropy is regularised" while
the III_1 matter algebra + modular Hamiltonian are RETAINED. A3 asks edge-

(iii) CIRCULARITY: the crossed product adjoins the clock PRECISELY to supply a

# Ledger — RUN 34, ARM A1 (THE LIFT) + A2 (THE DESCENT LIMIT)

(This file is the A1/A2 arm. ARM A3-cost runs concurrently and owns

(16/16 checks PASS, EXIT=0). ZERO writes to `unification.tex` / `ISSUES.md`.)

## COMMISSION
A1: write the SM n=1 Clausius identity (R-i) IN the Type-II crossed product
A_cp = A_W x|_sigma R; does the descent boundary term [x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0} become
the AFFILIATION DEFECT of a now-affiliated charge (=> zero defect => gate
closes), and at what ORDER does the closure live?
A2: does the Type-II finiteness DESCEND to the fixed-background hbar-order
charge as G->0 / N->infty (A_cp -> A_W)? Compute the N-scaling explicitly.

## VERDICT (one line)
**NEGATIVE-RESULT: the gap does NOT bridge.** The lift splits the boundary term
E_cp = E_bare + E_dress; the clock-adjunction piece E_dress is O(G)~O(1/N) and
IS the (vanishing) affiliation defect of the dressed Khat=K_omega+q, but the
piece the paper's n=1 clause consumes — E_bare — is O(G^0)/hbar-order,
UNTOUCHED by the dressing, and STILL DIVERGENT. The closure lives strictly at
G-ORDER (a gravitational correction to the first law); it does NOT descend to
the hbar-order object. The descent limit (A2) confirms this quantitatively: the
Type-II finiteness is N-dependent (clock resolution rho_clock~1/N~G) and
DISAPPEARS as N->infty (G->0), returning to the bare III_1 divergent value.
All three refuters (order-mismatch, wrong-object, circularity) LAND. This makes
precise, at the level of the Clausius identity, the paper's OWN recorded
position (unification l.19865-67): "the identity the paper's n=1 clause consumes
lives in the fixed-background algebra, so this does not close the gate; it
locates it invariantly."

Grade: **NEGATIVE-RESULT** with a THEOREM-in-model sub-result (the descent
boundary term IS the affiliation defect, order-graded, closing at G-order only)
and a clean order-audit (finiteness strictly O(G), no descent).

---

## THE STRUCTURE (what run-29 fixed; NOT re-litigated)
Run-29 ARM A2 (edge_corner.py) trichotomy, inherited BINDING:
 (I)   III_1 wedge A_W: one-sided boost K_omega NOT AFFILIATED (no trace);
       the runs 24-29 divergence is the quantitative shadow of non-affiliation.
 (II)  relative charge K(W_R)-K(W_L): edge cancels by boost-antisymmetry,
       FINITE — but the WRONG (relative) identity, not the one-sided one.
 (III) Type-II crossed product A_cp: dressed corner charge Khat=K_omega+q
       AFFILIATED, self-adjoint, FINITE (thm:gravity-realisation / CLPW). The
       G-order gravitational move (adjoin the ADM/clock dof).
Run-26 settled the gate object: E=[x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0}, Y^1 dim-3 gh#+1 ghost
bilinear, connected edge two-point on the rho^-4 dimensional floor; smeared
E[f]^2 ~ int rho^{2 alpha_f+2-2p}, FINITE iff alpha_f>p-3/2; paper consumer
alpha_f=0 => DIVERGES (KMS/Krein orderings). A3 = edge-locality of Y^1 at B.

## A1 — THE LIFT (tii_lift.py §A,B,D; tii_lift.tex §2)
The n=1 identity (R-i, unification l.19515/l.19752):
    delta K_omega^(1) = 2pi int_B x^1 delta<T_00^(1)>,  from the IBP
    2pi int_W x^1 partial(.) = 2pi int_B x^1 T_00 - [x^1 Y^1]_{x^1=0}.
Lift K_omega -> Khat = K_omega + q into A_cp. Boundary term splits additively:
    E_cp = E_bare + E_dress.
 - E_bare = [x^1 Y^1_bare]_{x^1=0}: O(G^0) (= hbar-order). Y^1_bare is the
   descent of the BARE boost stress, defined on A_W with NO reference to G/clock;
   edge two-point on the rho^-4 floor; smeared boundary DIVERGES for alpha_f=0.
   UNCHANGED by the lift. [py §B: E_bare ~ rho_min^-1, diverges as edge cutoff->0]
 - E_dress = [x^1 (q-moment descent)]_{x^1=0}: O(G)~O(1/N), FINITE. The clock q
   is positive s.a. on L^2(R_+), Dixmier-Malliavin SMOOTH, global observer/ADM
   dof, NO edge singularity at B; enters weighted by eps_grav ~ 1/N ~ G.
   IS the affiliation defect of Khat, and it VANISHES (Khat affiliated by Stone:
   e^{it Khat} in A_cp). [py §B: E_dress = eps_grav * O(1), finite]
KEY (Cor: no-close): a FINITE O(G) term cannot cancel a DIVERGENT O(G^0) term —
different orders in the gravitational expansion, one finite one not. The
affiliation of Khat removes the DRESSING defect E_dress; the paper's n=1 clause
consumes E_bare, untouched. Closure lives at G-ORDER; no descent to hbar-order.

THE AFFILIATION-DEFECT IDENTITY (py §D; tex Prop:defect), order-graded:
    O(G^0): E_bare  = aff-defect(K_omega^bare) != 0  (III_1 obstruction, ALL N)
    O(G^1): E_dress = aff-defect(q-dressing)  = 0    (Khat affiliated, Stone)
So "E = aff-defect(Khat) = 0 => gate closes" is TRUE only at O(G). At O(G^0) —
the order the paper's clause lives at — the defect is aff-defect(K_omega^bare)
!= 0, unchanged by the lift. [py §D: D_bare=0.9568 at N=1e2,1e6,1e122 alike]
=> THEOREM-in-model (the boundary term IS the affiliation defect, closing at
   G-order) but EMPTY for the paper's clause (correct object, WRONG order).
   The fourth cousin of the one-point/two-point transport trap (#32/33/34/36).

## A2 — THE DESCENT LIMIT (tii_lift.py §C,C'; tii_lift.tex §3)
N/G dictionary: Tr_r(1)=e^N, N=S_dS, G=3pi/(Lambda N) => N->infty <=> G->0
(Lambda fixed). The clock supplies a PHYSICAL edge regulator: the dressed charge
cannot resolve B sharper than the observer clock scale rho_clock ~ 1/N. So
    <corner>_Tr_r = int_{rho_clock}^1 x^1 ||Y^1|| drho ~ int_{1/N}^1 rho^-2 drho
                  ~ N  -> infinity as N->infty (G->0).
[py §C: <corner> ~ N^+1.00 across N=1e1..1e9; FINITE at each N, DIVERGES as N->inf]
=> The finiteness is a STRICTLY gravitational (O(G)) regularization, NOT a
   descent to a finite hbar-order object. As G->0 the clock decouples,
   A_cp -> A_W, and the finite value returns to the bare III_1 (divergent) value.
   A2 is NEGATIVE. This is the HONEST OBSTRUCTION (refuter i, quantitative).

VALUE-INVARIANCE ESCAPE, DISARMED (py §C'; tex Rem:value-escape). Run-31 proved
<Q_d>_rho = Tr_r(rho_hat Q_d) is INVARIANT under Tr_r -> lambda Tr_r (a ratio,
degree-0). ADVERSARY: "value N/lambda-independent => finiteness descends." NO:
run-31's invariance is of an ALREADY-AFFILIATED object's value; it presupposes
A_cp (=> G != 0). The ratio is defined only where Tr_r exists; as G->0, Tr_r
ceases to exist (III_1 no normal trace) and BOTH numerator and denominator
diverge (~N), the finite ratio a 0/0 artifact of the regulated algebra.
[py §C': ratio=0.317 for lambda in {1,1e6} (run-31 reproduced), num & den both
~N->inf.] Value-invariance (a ratio) is ORTHOGONAL to the descent question
(existence). Only the latter is the descent; it is negative. This also disarms
the circularity refuter's dual: the arm is NOT covertly assuming non-descent —
it exhibits that the one invariance run-31 proved is orthogonal to descent.
